built with pdoc

bimascode

BIM as Code - A Python library for programmatic Building Information Modeling.

 1"""
 2BIM as Code - A Python library for programmatic Building Information Modeling.
 3"""
 4
 5__version__ = "0.1.0"
 6
 7# Core classes
 8# Architecture
 9from .architecture import (
10    Floor,
11    FloorType,
12    Layer,
13    LayerFunction,
14    Roof,
15    Wall,
16    WallType,
17)
18from .core import Element, ElementInstance, ElementType
19
20# Spatial organization
21from .spatial import Building, Level
22
23# Units
24from .utils import (
25    Angle,
26    Area,
27    Length,
28    LengthUnit,
29    UnitSystem,
30    Volume,
31)
32
33__all__ = [
34    # Core
35    "Element",
36    "ElementType",
37    "ElementInstance",
38    # Spatial
39    "Building",
40    "Level",
41    # Architecture
42    "WallType",
43    "Wall",
44    "FloorType",
45    "Floor",
46    "Roof",
47    "Layer",
48    "LayerFunction",
49    # Units
50    "UnitSystem",
51    "LengthUnit",
52    "Length",
53    "Area",
54    "Volume",
55    "Angle",
56]
class Element: 
 13class Element:
 14    """
 15    Base class for all BIM elements.
 16
 17    Provides:
 18    - Unique GUID generation
 19    - Property storage
 20    - Common attributes (name, description)
 21    """
 22
 23    def __init__(self, name: str, description: str | None = None):
 24        """
 25        Initialize a BIM element.
 26
 27        Args:
 28            name: Element name
 29            description: Optional element description
 30        """
 31        self.name = name
 32        self.description = description
 33        self._guid = self._generate_guid()
 34        self._properties: dict[str, Any] = {}
 35        self._modified_timestamp: float = time.time()
 36        self._cached_2d: Any = None
 37        self._cache_timestamp: float = 0.0
 38
 39    @staticmethod
 40    def _generate_guid() -> str:
 41        """
 42        Generate a valid IFC GUID.
 43
 44        IFC GUIDs are base64-encoded UUIDs with a specific character set.
 45        For now, we use standard UUIDs and will convert during IFC export.
 46        """
 47        return str(uuid.uuid4())
 48
 49    @property
 50    def guid(self) -> str:
 51        """Get the element's globally unique identifier."""
 52        return self._guid
 53
 54    def set_property(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:
 55        """
 56        Set a custom property on the element.
 57
 58        Args:
 59            name: Property name
 60            value: Property value
 61        """
 62        self._properties[name] = value
 63
 64    def get_property(self, name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
 65        """
 66        Get a custom property value.
 67
 68        Args:
 69            name: Property name
 70            default: Default value if property not found
 71
 72        Returns:
 73            Property value or default
 74        """
 75        return self._properties.get(name, default)
 76
 77    @property
 78    def properties(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
 79        """Get all custom properties."""
 80        return self._properties.copy()
 81
 82    def __repr__(self) -> str:
 83        return f"{self.__class__.__name__}(name='{self.name}', guid='{self._guid}')"
 84
 85    def _invalidate_cache(self) -> None:
 86        """Invalidate cached representations when geometry changes.
 87
 88        Call this method from property setters that modify element geometry.
 89        """
 90        self._modified_timestamp = time.time()
 91        self._cached_2d = None
 92
 93    @property
 94    def modified_timestamp(self) -> float:
 95        """Get the timestamp of the last geometry modification."""
 96        return self._modified_timestamp
 97
 98    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox | None:
 99        """Get the axis-aligned bounding box of this element.
100
101        Subclasses should override this method to provide accurate bounds.
102
103        Returns:
104            BoundingBox for the element, or None if not applicable
105        """
106        return None

Base class for all BIM elements.

Provides:

  • Unique GUID generation
  • Property storage
  • Common attributes (name, description)
Element(name: str, description: str | None = None) 
23    def __init__(self, name: str, description: str | None = None):
24        """
25        Initialize a BIM element.
26
27        Args:
28            name: Element name
29            description: Optional element description
30        """
31        self.name = name
32        self.description = description
33        self._guid = self._generate_guid()
34        self._properties: dict[str, Any] = {}
35        self._modified_timestamp: float = time.time()
36        self._cached_2d: Any = None
37        self._cache_timestamp: float = 0.0

Initialize a BIM element.

Args: name: Element name description: Optional element description

def get_bounding_box(self) -> bimascode.performance.bounding_box.BoundingBox | None: 
 98    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox | None:
 99        """Get the axis-aligned bounding box of this element.
100
101        Subclasses should override this method to provide accurate bounds.
102
103        Returns:
104            BoundingBox for the element, or None if not applicable
105        """
106        return None

Get the axis-aligned bounding box of this element.

Subclasses should override this method to provide accurate bounds.

Returns: BoundingBox for the element, or None if not applicable

class ElementType(abc.ABC): 
 17class ElementType(ABC):
 18    """
 19    Base class for all element types (WallType, FloorType, etc.).
 20
 21    Types define shared parameters that propagate to all instances.
 22    When a type parameter changes, all instances are notified.
 23    """
 24
 25    def __init__(self, name: str):
 26        """
 27        Initialize an element type.
 28
 29        Args:
 30            name: Unique name for this type
 31        """
 32        self.name = name
 33        self.guid = str(uuid.uuid4())
 34        self._instances: list[ElementInstance] = []
 35        self._type_parameters: dict[str, Any] = {}
 36
 37    def set_parameter(self, param_name: str, value: Any) -> None:
 38        """
 39        Set a type parameter value.
 40
 41        This will notify all instances that the type parameter has changed.
 42        Instances without overrides will reflect the new value.
 43
 44        Args:
 45            param_name: Name of the parameter
 46            value: New value for the parameter
 47        """
 48        old_value = self._type_parameters.get(param_name)
 49        self._type_parameters[param_name] = value
 50
 51        # Notify all instances of the change
 52        for instance in self._instances:
 53            instance._on_type_parameter_changed(param_name, value, old_value)
 54
 55    def get_parameter(self, param_name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
 56        """
 57        Get a type parameter value.
 58
 59        Args:
 60            param_name: Name of the parameter
 61            default: Default value if parameter doesn't exist
 62
 63        Returns:
 64            Parameter value or default
 65        """
 66        return self._type_parameters.get(param_name, default)
 67
 68    def _register_instance(self, instance: "ElementInstance") -> None:
 69        """Register an instance with this type."""
 70        if instance not in self._instances:
 71            self._instances.append(instance)
 72
 73    def _unregister_instance(self, instance: "ElementInstance") -> None:
 74        """Unregister an instance from this type."""
 75        if instance in self._instances:
 76            self._instances.remove(instance)
 77
 78    @property
 79    def instances(self) -> list["ElementInstance"]:
 80        """Get all instances of this type."""
 81        return self._instances.copy()
 82
 83    @property
 84    def instance_count(self) -> int:
 85        """Get the number of instances of this type."""
 86        return len(self._instances)
 87
 88    @abstractmethod
 89    def create_geometry(self, instance: "ElementInstance") -> Any:
 90        """
 91        Create geometry for an instance of this type.
 92
 93        This method must be implemented by subclasses to define how
 94        geometry is created based on type and instance parameters.
 95
 96        Args:
 97            instance: The instance to create geometry for
 98
 99        Returns:
100            Geometry object (typically build123d solid)
101        """
102        pass
103
104    def __repr__(self) -> str:
105        return f"{self.__class__.__name__}(name='{self.name}', instances={self.instance_count})"

Base class for all element types (WallType, FloorType, etc.).

Types define shared parameters that propagate to all instances. When a type parameter changes, all instances are notified.

ElementType(name: str)

26        """
27        Initialize an element type.
28
29        Args:
30            name: Unique name for this type
31        """
32        self.name = name
33        self.guid = str(uuid.uuid4())
34        self._instances: list[ElementInstance] = []
35        self._type_parameters: dict[str, Any] = {}

Initialize an element type.

Args: name: Unique name for this type

def set_parameter(self, param_name: str, value: Any) -> None: 
37    def set_parameter(self, param_name: str, value: Any) -> None:
38        """
39        Set a type parameter value.
40
41        This will notify all instances that the type parameter has changed.
42        Instances without overrides will reflect the new value.
43
44        Args:
45            param_name: Name of the parameter
46            value: New value for the parameter
47        """
48        old_value = self._type_parameters.get(param_name)
49        self._type_parameters[param_name] = value
50
51        # Notify all instances of the change
52        for instance in self._instances:
53            instance._on_type_parameter_changed(param_name, value, old_value)

56        """
57        Get a type parameter value.
58
59        Args:
60            param_name: Name of the parameter
61            default: Default value if parameter doesn't exist

64            Parameter value or default

66        return self._type_parameters.get(param_name, default)

@abstractmethod
def create_geometry(self, instance: ElementInstance) -> Any: 
 88    @abstractmethod
 89    def create_geometry(self, instance: "ElementInstance") -> Any:
 90        """
 91        Create geometry for an instance of this type.
 92
 93        This method must be implemented by subclasses to define how
 94        geometry is created based on type and instance parameters.
 95
 96        Args:
 97            instance: The instance to create geometry for
 98
 99        Returns:
100            Geometry object (typically build123d solid)
101        """
102        pass

Create geometry for an instance of this type.

This method must be implemented by subclasses to define how geometry is created based on type and instance parameters.

Args: instance: The instance to create geometry for

Returns: Geometry object (typically build123d solid)

class ElementInstance(abc.ABC): 
108class ElementInstance(ABC):
109    """
110    Base class for all element instances (Wall, Floor, etc.).
111
112    Instances reference a type and can override type parameters with
113    instance-specific values.
114    """
115
116    def __init__(self, element_type: ElementType, name: str | None = None):
117        """
118        Initialize an element instance.
119
120        Args:
121            element_type: The type this instance belongs to
122            name: Optional unique name for this instance
123        """
124        self.type = element_type
125        self.name = name or f"{element_type.name}_{len(element_type.instances) + 1}"
126        self.guid = str(uuid.uuid4())
127
128        self._instance_parameters: dict[str, Any] = {}
129        self._overridden_parameters: set[str] = set()
130        self._geometry = None
131        self._geometry_valid = False
132
133        # Cache invalidation support (Sprint 5)
134        self._modified_timestamp: float = time.time()
135        self._cached_2d: Any = None
136        self._cache_timestamp: float = 0.0
137
138        # Register with type
139        self.type._register_instance(self)
140
141    def set_parameter(self, param_name: str, value: Any, override: bool = True) -> None:
142        """
143        Set an instance parameter value.
144
145        Args:
146            param_name: Name of the parameter
147            value: New value for the parameter
148            override: If True, this overrides the type parameter
149        """
150        self._instance_parameters[param_name] = value
151
152        if override:
153            self._overridden_parameters.add(param_name)
154
155        # Invalidate geometry when parameters change
156        self._geometry_valid = False
157
158    def get_parameter(self, param_name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
159        """
160        Get a parameter value.
161
162        This will first check for instance overrides, then fall back to
163        the type parameter.
164
165        Args:
166            param_name: Name of the parameter
167            default: Default value if parameter doesn't exist
168
169        Returns:
170            Parameter value or default
171        """
172        # Check for instance override first
173        if param_name in self._overridden_parameters:
174            return self._instance_parameters.get(param_name, default)
175
176        # Fall back to type parameter
177        type_value = self.type.get_parameter(param_name)
178        if type_value is not None:
179            return type_value
180
181        # Finally check instance parameters (non-override)
182        return self._instance_parameters.get(param_name, default)
183
184    def reset_parameter(self, param_name: str) -> None:
185        """
186        Reset a parameter to use the type value.
187
188        This removes any instance override for the parameter.
189
190        Args:
191            param_name: Name of the parameter to reset
192        """
193        if param_name in self._overridden_parameters:
194            self._overridden_parameters.remove(param_name)
195            self._geometry_valid = False
196
197    def is_parameter_overridden(self, param_name: str) -> bool:
198        """
199        Check if a parameter is overridden at the instance level.
200
201        Args:
202            param_name: Name of the parameter
203
204        Returns:
205            True if parameter is overridden
206        """
207        return param_name in self._overridden_parameters
208
209    @staticmethod
210    def _generate_guid() -> str:
211        """
212        Generate a valid IFC GUID.
213
214        Returns:
215            UUID string
216        """
217        return str(uuid.uuid4())
218
219    def _on_type_parameter_changed(self, param_name: str, new_value: Any, old_value: Any) -> None:
220        """
221        Called when a type parameter changes.
222
223        This only affects the instance if the parameter is not overridden.
224
225        Args:
226            param_name: Name of the changed parameter
227            new_value: New parameter value
228            old_value: Previous parameter value
229        """
230        if param_name not in self._overridden_parameters:
231            # Parameter is not overridden, so instance is affected
232            self._geometry_valid = False
233
234    def invalidate_geometry(self) -> None:
235        """Mark geometry as invalid and requiring regeneration.
236
237        Also invalidates the 2D representation cache.
238        """
239        self._geometry_valid = False
240        self._invalidate_cache()
241
242    def _invalidate_cache(self) -> None:
243        """Invalidate cached representations when geometry changes."""
244        self._modified_timestamp = time.time()
245        self._cached_2d = None
246
247    @property
248    def modified_timestamp(self) -> float:
249        """Get the timestamp of the last geometry modification."""
250        return self._modified_timestamp
251
252    def get_geometry(self, force_rebuild: bool = False) -> Any:
253        """
254        Get the geometry for this instance.
255
256        Geometry is cached and only regenerated when invalid.
257
258        Args:
259            force_rebuild: Force geometry regeneration even if valid
260
261        Returns:
262            Geometry object (typically build123d solid)
263        """
264        if not self._geometry_valid or force_rebuild:
265            self._geometry = self.type.create_geometry(self)
266            self._geometry_valid = True
267
268        return self._geometry
269
270    @property
271    def overridden_parameters(self) -> set[str]:
272        """Get the set of parameters overridden at instance level."""
273        return self._overridden_parameters.copy()
274
275    def __repr__(self) -> str:
276        return f"{self.__class__.__name__}(name='{self.name}', type='{self.type.name}')"

Base class for all element instances (Wall, Floor, etc.).

Instances reference a type and can override type parameters with instance-specific values.

ElementInstance( element_type: ElementType, name: str | None = None) 
116    def __init__(self, element_type: ElementType, name: str | None = None):
117        """
118        Initialize an element instance.
119
120        Args:
121            element_type: The type this instance belongs to
122            name: Optional unique name for this instance
123        """
124        self.type = element_type
125        self.name = name or f"{element_type.name}_{len(element_type.instances) + 1}"
126        self.guid = str(uuid.uuid4())
127
128        self._instance_parameters: dict[str, Any] = {}
129        self._overridden_parameters: set[str] = set()
130        self._geometry = None
131        self._geometry_valid = False
132
133        # Cache invalidation support (Sprint 5)
134        self._modified_timestamp: float = time.time()
135        self._cached_2d: Any = None
136        self._cache_timestamp: float = 0.0
137
138        # Register with type
139        self.type._register_instance(self)

def get_parameter(self, param_name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any: 
158    def get_parameter(self, param_name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
159        """
160        Get a parameter value.
161
162        This will first check for instance overrides, then fall back to
163        the type parameter.
164
165        Args:
166            param_name: Name of the parameter
167            default: Default value if parameter doesn't exist
168
169        Returns:
170            Parameter value or default
171        """
172        # Check for instance override first
173        if param_name in self._overridden_parameters:
174            return self._instance_parameters.get(param_name, default)
175
176        # Fall back to type parameter
177        type_value = self.type.get_parameter(param_name)
178        if type_value is not None:
179            return type_value
180
181        # Finally check instance parameters (non-override)
182        return self._instance_parameters.get(param_name, default)

Get a parameter value.

This will first check for instance overrides, then fall back to the type parameter.

Args: param_name: Name of the parameter default: Default value if parameter doesn't exist

Returns: Parameter value or default

def reset_parameter(self, param_name: str) -> None: 
184    def reset_parameter(self, param_name: str) -> None:
185        """
186        Reset a parameter to use the type value.
187
188        This removes any instance override for the parameter.
189
190        Args:
191            param_name: Name of the parameter to reset
192        """
193        if param_name in self._overridden_parameters:
194            self._overridden_parameters.remove(param_name)
195            self._geometry_valid = False

Reset a parameter to use the type value.

This removes any instance override for the parameter.

Args: param_name: Name of the parameter to reset

def is_parameter_overridden(self, param_name: str) -> bool: 
197    def is_parameter_overridden(self, param_name: str) -> bool:
198        """
199        Check if a parameter is overridden at the instance level.
200
201        Args:
202            param_name: Name of the parameter
203
204        Returns:
205            True if parameter is overridden
206        """
207        return param_name in self._overridden_parameters

Check if a parameter is overridden at the instance level.

Args: param_name: Name of the parameter

Returns: True if parameter is overridden

def invalidate_geometry(self) -> None: 
234    def invalidate_geometry(self) -> None:
235        """Mark geometry as invalid and requiring regeneration.
236
237        Also invalidates the 2D representation cache.
238        """
239        self._geometry_valid = False
240        self._invalidate_cache()

Mark geometry as invalid and requiring regeneration.

Also invalidates the 2D representation cache.

modified_timestamp: float 
247    @property
248    def modified_timestamp(self) -> float:
249        """Get the timestamp of the last geometry modification."""
250        return self._modified_timestamp

Get the timestamp of the last geometry modification.

def get_geometry(self, force_rebuild: bool = False) -> Any: 
252    def get_geometry(self, force_rebuild: bool = False) -> Any:
253        """
254        Get the geometry for this instance.
255
256        Geometry is cached and only regenerated when invalid.
257
258        Args:
259            force_rebuild: Force geometry regeneration even if valid
260
261        Returns:
262            Geometry object (typically build123d solid)
263        """
264        if not self._geometry_valid or force_rebuild:
265            self._geometry = self.type.create_geometry(self)
266            self._geometry_valid = True
267
268        return self._geometry

Get the geometry for this instance.

Geometry is cached and only regenerated when invalid.

Args: force_rebuild: Force geometry regeneration even if valid

Returns: Geometry object (typically build123d solid)

overridden_parameters: set[str] 
270    @property
271    def overridden_parameters(self) -> set[str]:
272        """Get the set of parameters overridden at instance level."""
273        return self._overridden_parameters.copy()

Get the set of parameters overridden at instance level.

class Building(bimascode.Element): 
 12class Building(Element):
 13    """
 14    Represents the root building object and IFC project hierarchy.
 15
 16    Creates the full IFC scaffolding:
 17    IfcProject → IfcSite → IfcBuilding → IfcBuildingStorey (levels)
 18    """
 19
 20    def __init__(
 21        self,
 22        name: str,
 23        address: str | None = None,
 24        description: str | None = None,
 25        unit_system: str | UnitSystem = UnitSystem.METRIC,
 26    ):
 27        """
 28        Create a new building.
 29
 30        Args:
 31            name: Building name
 32            address: Building address
 33            description: Optional description
 34            unit_system: Unit system to use ('metric' or 'imperial')
 35        """
 36        super().__init__(name=name, description=description)
 37
 38        self.address = address
 39
 40        # Unit system
 41        if isinstance(unit_system, str):
 42            unit_system = UnitSystem(unit_system)
 43        self._unit_system = unit_system
 44
 45        # Set default length unit based on system
 46        if unit_system == UnitSystem.METRIC:
 47            self._length_unit = LengthUnit.MILLIMETER
 48        else:
 49            self._length_unit = LengthUnit.INCH
 50
 51        # Collections
 52        self._levels: list[Level] = []  # noqa: F821
 53        self._grids: list = []
 54        self._elements: list = []
 55
 56        # IFC entities (populated during export)
 57        self._ifc_file = None
 58        self._ifc_project = None
 59        self._ifc_site = None
 60        self._ifc_building = None
 61        self._ifc_owner_history = None
 62
 63    @property
 64    def unit_system(self) -> UnitSystem:
 65        """Get the building's unit system."""
 66        return self._unit_system
 67
 68    @property
 69    def length_unit(self) -> LengthUnit:
 70        """Get the default length unit for this building."""
 71        return self._length_unit
 72
 73    @property
 74    def levels(self) -> list["Level"]:  # noqa: F821
 75        """Get all levels in the building."""
 76        return self._levels.copy()
 77
 78    @property
 79    def grids(self) -> list:
 80        """Get all grid lines in the building."""
 81        return self._grids.copy()
 82
 83    def _add_level(self, level: "Level") -> None:  # noqa: F821
 84        """
 85        Internal method to register a level with the building.
 86
 87        Args:
 88            level: Level to add
 89        """
 90        if level not in self._levels:
 91            self._levels.append(level)
 92
 93    def get_level(self, name: str) -> Optional["Level"]:  # noqa: F821
 94        """
 95        Get a level by name.
 96
 97        Args:
 98            name: Level name
 99
100        Returns:
101            Level if found, None otherwise
102        """
103        for level in self._levels:
104            if level.name == name:
105                return level
106        return None
107
108    @classmethod
109    def from_ifc(cls, filepath: str) -> "Building":
110        """
111        Create a Building instance by importing from an IFC file.
112
113        Args:
114            filepath: Path to IFC file
115
116        Returns:
117            Building instance with imported elements
118
119        Raises:
120            ImportError: If ifcopenshell is not installed
121            FileNotFoundError: If IFC file doesn't exist
122            ValueError: If IFC file has invalid structure
123        """
124        from ..export.ifc_importer import IFCImporter
125
126        importer = IFCImporter()
127        return importer.import_building(filepath)
128
129    def export_ifc(self, filepath: str, schema: str = "IFC4") -> None:
130        """
131        Export the building to an IFC file.
132
133        Args:
134            filepath: Output file path
135            schema: IFC schema version (default: IFC4)
136        """
137        from ..export.ifc_exporter import IFCExporter
138
139        exporter = IFCExporter(schema=schema)
140        exporter.export(self, filepath)
141
142    def get_rooms(self) -> list:
143        """
144        Get all rooms in the building across all levels.
145
146        Returns:
147            List of Room objects
148        """
149        from ..spatial.room import Room
150
151        rooms = []
152        for level in self._levels:
153            for element in level.elements:
154                if isinstance(element, Room):
155                    rooms.append(element)
156        return rooms
157
158    def room_schedule(self):
159        """
160        Generate a room schedule as a pandas DataFrame.
161
162        Returns a DataFrame with columns:
163        - number: Room number
164        - name: Room name
165        - level: Level name
166        - area_m2: Area in square meters
167        - area_sqft: Area in square feet
168        - volume_m3: Volume in cubic meters
169        - height_m: Floor-to-ceiling height in meters
170        - perimeter_m: Perimeter in meters
171        - floor_finish: Floor finish description
172        - wall_finish: Wall finish description
173        - ceiling_finish: Ceiling finish description
174
175        Returns:
176            pandas DataFrame with room schedule
177
178        Raises:
179            ImportError: If pandas is not installed
180        """
181        try:
182            import pandas as pd
183        except ImportError:
184            raise ImportError(
185                "pandas is required for room_schedule(). Install with: pip install pandas"
186            )
187
188        rooms = self.get_rooms()
189
190        if not rooms:
191            # Return empty DataFrame with correct columns
192            return pd.DataFrame(
193                columns=[
194                    "number",
195                    "name",
196                    "level",
197                    "area_m2",
198                    "area_sqft",
199                    "volume_m3",
200                    "height_m",
201                    "perimeter_m",
202                    "floor_finish",
203                    "wall_finish",
204                    "ceiling_finish",
205                ]
206            )
207
208        # Convert rooms to dictionaries
209        data = [room.to_dict() for room in rooms]
210
211        # Create DataFrame
212        df = pd.DataFrame(data)
213
214        # Sort by level and room number
215        df = df.sort_values(by=["level", "number"])
216
217        return df
218
219    def __repr__(self) -> str:
220        return f"Building(name='{self.name}', levels={len(self._levels)})"

Represents the root building object and IFC project hierarchy.

Creates the full IFC scaffolding: IfcProject → IfcSite → IfcBuilding → IfcBuildingStorey (levels)

Building( name: str, address: str | None = None, description: str | None = None, unit_system: str | UnitSystem = <UnitSystem.METRIC: 'metric'>) 
20    def __init__(
21        self,
22        name: str,
23        address: str | None = None,
24        description: str | None = None,
25        unit_system: str | UnitSystem = UnitSystem.METRIC,
26    ):
27        """
28        Create a new building.
29
30        Args:
31            name: Building name
32            address: Building address
33            description: Optional description
34            unit_system: Unit system to use ('metric' or 'imperial')
35        """
36        super().__init__(name=name, description=description)
37
38        self.address = address
39
40        # Unit system
41        if isinstance(unit_system, str):
42            unit_system = UnitSystem(unit_system)
43        self._unit_system = unit_system
44
45        # Set default length unit based on system
46        if unit_system == UnitSystem.METRIC:
47            self._length_unit = LengthUnit.MILLIMETER
48        else:
49            self._length_unit = LengthUnit.INCH
50
51        # Collections
52        self._levels: list[Level] = []  # noqa: F821
53        self._grids: list = []
54        self._elements: list = []
55
56        # IFC entities (populated during export)
57        self._ifc_file = None
58        self._ifc_project = None
59        self._ifc_site = None
60        self._ifc_building = None
61        self._ifc_owner_history = None

Create a new building.

Args: name: Building name address: Building address description: Optional description unit_system: Unit system to use ('metric' or 'imperial')

address
unit_system: UnitSystem 
63    @property
64    def unit_system(self) -> UnitSystem:
65        """Get the building's unit system."""
66        return self._unit_system

Get the building's unit system.

length_unit: LengthUnit 
68    @property
69    def length_unit(self) -> LengthUnit:
70        """Get the default length unit for this building."""
71        return self._length_unit

Get the default length unit for this building.

levels: list['Level'] 
73    @property
74    def levels(self) -> list["Level"]:  # noqa: F821
75        """Get all levels in the building."""
76        return self._levels.copy()

Get all levels in the building.

grids: list 
78    @property
79    def grids(self) -> list:
80        """Get all grid lines in the building."""
81        return self._grids.copy()

Get all grid lines in the building.

def get_level(self, name: str) -> Optional[ForwardRef('Level')]: 
 93    def get_level(self, name: str) -> Optional["Level"]:  # noqa: F821
 94        """
 95        Get a level by name.
 96
 97        Args:
 98            name: Level name
 99
100        Returns:
101            Level if found, None otherwise
102        """
103        for level in self._levels:
104            if level.name == name:
105                return level
106        return None

Get a level by name.

Args: name: Level name

Returns: Level if found, None otherwise

@classmethod
def from_ifc(cls, filepath: str) -> Building: 
108    @classmethod
109    def from_ifc(cls, filepath: str) -> "Building":
110        """
111        Create a Building instance by importing from an IFC file.
112
113        Args:
114            filepath: Path to IFC file
115
116        Returns:
117            Building instance with imported elements
118
119        Raises:
120            ImportError: If ifcopenshell is not installed
121            FileNotFoundError: If IFC file doesn't exist
122            ValueError: If IFC file has invalid structure
123        """
124        from ..export.ifc_importer import IFCImporter
125
126        importer = IFCImporter()
127        return importer.import_building(filepath)

Returns: Building instance with imported elements

Raises: ImportError: If ifcopenshell is not installed FileNotFoundError: If IFC file doesn't exist ValueError: If IFC file has invalid structure

def export_ifc(self, filepath: str, schema: str = 'IFC4') -> None: 
129    def export_ifc(self, filepath: str, schema: str = "IFC4") -> None:
130        """
131        Export the building to an IFC file.
132
133        Args:
134            filepath: Output file path
135            schema: IFC schema version (default: IFC4)
136        """
137        from ..export.ifc_exporter import IFCExporter
138
139        exporter = IFCExporter(schema=schema)
140        exporter.export(self, filepath)

Export the building to an IFC file.

def get_rooms(self) -> list: 
142    def get_rooms(self) -> list:
143        """
144        Get all rooms in the building across all levels.
145
146        Returns:
147            List of Room objects
148        """
149        from ..spatial.room import Room
150
151        rooms = []
152        for level in self._levels:
153            for element in level.elements:
154                if isinstance(element, Room):
155                    rooms.append(element)
156        return rooms

Get all rooms in the building across all levels.

Returns: List of Room objects

def room_schedule(self): 
158    def room_schedule(self):
159        """
160        Generate a room schedule as a pandas DataFrame.
161
162        Returns a DataFrame with columns:
163        - number: Room number
164        - name: Room name
165        - level: Level name
166        - area_m2: Area in square meters
167        - area_sqft: Area in square feet
168        - volume_m3: Volume in cubic meters
169        - height_m: Floor-to-ceiling height in meters
170        - perimeter_m: Perimeter in meters
171        - floor_finish: Floor finish description
172        - wall_finish: Wall finish description
173        - ceiling_finish: Ceiling finish description
174
175        Returns:
176            pandas DataFrame with room schedule
177
178        Raises:
179            ImportError: If pandas is not installed
180        """
181        try:
182            import pandas as pd
183        except ImportError:
184            raise ImportError(
185                "pandas is required for room_schedule(). Install with: pip install pandas"
186            )
187
188        rooms = self.get_rooms()
189
190        if not rooms:
191            # Return empty DataFrame with correct columns
192            return pd.DataFrame(
193                columns=[
194                    "number",
195                    "name",
196                    "level",
197                    "area_m2",
198                    "area_sqft",
199                    "volume_m3",
200                    "height_m",
201                    "perimeter_m",
202                    "floor_finish",
203                    "wall_finish",
204                    "ceiling_finish",
205                ]
206            )
207
208        # Convert rooms to dictionaries
209        data = [room.to_dict() for room in rooms]
210
211        # Create DataFrame
212        df = pd.DataFrame(data)
213
214        # Sort by level and room number
215        df = df.sort_values(by=["level", "number"])
216
217        return df

Generate a room schedule as a pandas DataFrame.

Returns a DataFrame with columns:

  • number: Room number
  • name: Room name
  • level: Level name
  • area_m2: Area in square meters
  • area_sqft: Area in square feet
  • volume_m3: Volume in cubic meters
  • height_m: Floor-to-ceiling height in meters
  • perimeter_m: Perimeter in meters
  • floor_finish: Floor finish description
  • wall_finish: Wall finish description
  • ceiling_finish: Ceiling finish description

Returns: pandas DataFrame with room schedule

Raises: ImportError: If pandas is not installed

class Level(bimascode.Element): 
 15class Level(Element):
 16    """
 17    Represents a horizontal datum plane (building storey) in the BIM model.
 18
 19    Every element in the model must be associated with a level.
 20    Maps to IfcBuildingStorey in IFC export.
 21    """
 22
 23    def __init__(
 24        self,
 25        building: "Building",
 26        name: str,
 27        elevation: float | Length,
 28        description: str | None = None,
 29    ):
 30        """
 31        Create a new level.
 32
 33        Args:
 34            building: Parent building
 35            name: Level name (e.g., "Ground Floor", "Level 1")
 36            elevation: Elevation above project base point
 37            description: Optional description
 38        """
 39        super().__init__(name=name, description=description)
 40
 41        self.building = building
 42        self._elevation = normalize_length(elevation, building.length_unit)
 43        self._elements = []  # Elements hosted on this level
 44
 45        # Register with building
 46        building._add_level(self)
 47
 48    @property
 49    def elevation(self) -> Length:
 50        """Get the level elevation as a Length object."""
 51        return self._elevation
 52
 53    @property
 54    def elevation_mm(self) -> float:
 55        """Get the level elevation in millimeters."""
 56        return self._elevation.mm
 57
 58    def set_elevation(self, elevation: float | Length) -> None:
 59        """
 60        Set the level elevation.
 61
 62        Args:
 63            elevation: New elevation value
 64        """
 65        self._elevation = normalize_length(elevation, self.building.length_unit)
 66
 67    def add_element(self, element) -> None:
 68        """
 69        Add an element to this level.
 70
 71        Args:
 72            element: Element to add (Wall, Floor, etc.)
 73        """
 74        if element not in self._elements:
 75            self._elements.append(element)
 76
 77    @property
 78    def elements(self):
 79        """Get all elements on this level."""
 80        return self._elements.copy()
 81
 82    def get_walls(self):
 83        """Get all walls on this level."""
 84        from bimascode.architecture.wall import Wall
 85
 86        return [e for e in self._elements if isinstance(e, Wall)]
 87
 88    def process_wall_joins(self, end_cap_type=None):
 89        """
 90        Process wall joins for all walls on this level.
 91
 92        Detects wall joins (corners, T-junctions, crosses) and calculates
 93        trim adjustments for clean connections.
 94
 95        Args:
 96            end_cap_type: How to trim wall ends (default: FLUSH)
 97                         Use EndCapType.EXTERIOR to extend walls to outer face
 98                         Use EndCapType.INTERIOR to trim to inner face
 99        """
100        from bimascode.architecture.wall_joins import EndCapType, detect_and_process_wall_joins
101
102        if end_cap_type is None:
103            end_cap_type = EndCapType.FLUSH
104
105        walls = self.get_walls()
106        if not walls:
107            return
108
109        # Detect and process joins
110        adjustments = detect_and_process_wall_joins(walls, end_cap_type)
111
112        # Apply adjustments to walls
113        for wall, adj in adjustments.items():
114            wall._trim_adjustments = adj
115            wall.invalidate_geometry()
116
117    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file):
118        """
119        Export this level to IFC as IfcBuildingStorey.
120
121        Args:
122            ifc_file: IfcOpenShell file object
123
124        Returns:
125            IfcBuildingStorey instance
126        """
127        # Create placement at elevation
128        elevation_mm = self.elevation_mm
129        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((0.0, 0.0, elevation_mm))
130        axis = ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0))
131        ref_direction = ifc_file.createIfcDirection((1.0, 0.0, 0.0))
132
133        axis3d = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(location, axis, ref_direction)
134        placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
135            (
136                self.building._ifc_building.ObjectPlacement
137                if hasattr(self.building, "_ifc_building")
138                else None
139            ),
140            axis3d,
141        )
142
143        # Create building storey
144        storey = ifc_file.createIfcBuildingStorey(
145            self.guid,
146            self.building._ifc_owner_history,
147            self.name,
148            self.description,
149            None,  # ObjectType
150            placement,
151            None,  # Representation
152            None,  # LongName
153            None,  # CompositionType
154            elevation_mm,  # Elevation
155        )
156
157        # Add to building's spatial structure
158        ifc_file.createIfcRelAggregates(
159            self._generate_guid(),
160            self.building._ifc_owner_history,
161            f"Building{self.building.name}Container",
162            None,
163            self.building._ifc_building,
164            [storey],
165        )
166
167        return storey
168
169    def __repr__(self) -> str:
170        return f"Level(name='{self.name}', elevation={self.elevation_mm}mm)"

Represents a horizontal datum plane (building storey) in the BIM model.

Every element in the model must be associated with a level. Maps to IfcBuildingStorey in IFC export.

Level( building: Building, name: str, elevation: float | Length, description: str | None = None) 
23    def __init__(
24        self,
25        building: "Building",
26        name: str,
27        elevation: float | Length,
28        description: str | None = None,
29    ):
30        """
31        Create a new level.
32
33        Args:
34            building: Parent building
35            name: Level name (e.g., "Ground Floor", "Level 1")
36            elevation: Elevation above project base point
37            description: Optional description
38        """
39        super().__init__(name=name, description=description)
40
41        self.building = building
42        self._elevation = normalize_length(elevation, building.length_unit)
43        self._elements = []  # Elements hosted on this level
44
45        # Register with building
46        building._add_level(self)

Create a new level.

Args: building: Parent building name: Level name (e.g., "Ground Floor", "Level 1") elevation: Elevation above project base point description: Optional description

building
elevation: Length 
48    @property
49    def elevation(self) -> Length:
50        """Get the level elevation as a Length object."""
51        return self._elevation

Get the level elevation as a Length object.

elevation_mm: float 
53    @property
54    def elevation_mm(self) -> float:
55        """Get the level elevation in millimeters."""
56        return self._elevation.mm

Get the level elevation in millimeters.

def set_elevation(self, elevation: float | Length) -> None: 
58    def set_elevation(self, elevation: float | Length) -> None:
59        """
60        Set the level elevation.
61
62        Args:
63            elevation: New elevation value
64        """
65        self._elevation = normalize_length(elevation, self.building.length_unit)

Set the level elevation.

Args: elevation: New elevation value

def add_element(self, element) -> None: 
67    def add_element(self, element) -> None:
68        """
69        Add an element to this level.
70
71        Args:
72            element: Element to add (Wall, Floor, etc.)
73        """
74        if element not in self._elements:
75            self._elements.append(element)

Add an element to this level.

elements 
77    @property
78    def elements(self):
79        """Get all elements on this level."""
80        return self._elements.copy()

Get all elements on this level.

def get_walls(self): 
82    def get_walls(self):
83        """Get all walls on this level."""
84        from bimascode.architecture.wall import Wall
85
86        return [e for e in self._elements if isinstance(e, Wall)]

Get all walls on this level.

def process_wall_joins(self, end_cap_type=None): 
 88    def process_wall_joins(self, end_cap_type=None):
 89        """
 90        Process wall joins for all walls on this level.
 91
 92        Detects wall joins (corners, T-junctions, crosses) and calculates
 93        trim adjustments for clean connections.
 94
 95        Args:
 96            end_cap_type: How to trim wall ends (default: FLUSH)
 97                         Use EndCapType.EXTERIOR to extend walls to outer face
 98                         Use EndCapType.INTERIOR to trim to inner face
 99        """
100        from bimascode.architecture.wall_joins import EndCapType, detect_and_process_wall_joins
101
102        if end_cap_type is None:
103            end_cap_type = EndCapType.FLUSH
104
105        walls = self.get_walls()
106        if not walls:
107            return
108
109        # Detect and process joins
110        adjustments = detect_and_process_wall_joins(walls, end_cap_type)
111
112        # Apply adjustments to walls
113        for wall, adj in adjustments.items():
114            wall._trim_adjustments = adj
115            wall.invalidate_geometry()

Process wall joins for all walls on this level.

Detects wall joins (corners, T-junctions, crosses) and calculates trim adjustments for clean connections.

Args: end_cap_type: How to trim wall ends (default: FLUSH) Use EndCapType.EXTERIOR to extend walls to outer face Use EndCapType.INTERIOR to trim to inner face

def to_ifc(self, ifc_file): 
117    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file):
118        """
119        Export this level to IFC as IfcBuildingStorey.
120
121        Args:
122            ifc_file: IfcOpenShell file object
123
124        Returns:
125            IfcBuildingStorey instance
126        """
127        # Create placement at elevation
128        elevation_mm = self.elevation_mm
129        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((0.0, 0.0, elevation_mm))
130        axis = ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0))
131        ref_direction = ifc_file.createIfcDirection((1.0, 0.0, 0.0))
132
133        axis3d = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(location, axis, ref_direction)
134        placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
135            (
136                self.building._ifc_building.ObjectPlacement
137                if hasattr(self.building, "_ifc_building")
138                else None
139            ),
140            axis3d,
141        )
142
143        # Create building storey
144        storey = ifc_file.createIfcBuildingStorey(
145            self.guid,
146            self.building._ifc_owner_history,
147            self.name,
148            self.description,
149            None,  # ObjectType
150            placement,
151            None,  # Representation
152            None,  # LongName
153            None,  # CompositionType
154            elevation_mm,  # Elevation
155        )
156
157        # Add to building's spatial structure
158        ifc_file.createIfcRelAggregates(
159            self._generate_guid(),
160            self.building._ifc_owner_history,
161            f"Building{self.building.name}Container",
162            None,
163            self.building._ifc_building,
164            [storey],
165        )
166
167        return storey

Export this level to IFC as IfcBuildingStorey.

Args: ifc_file: IfcOpenShell file object

Returns: IfcBuildingStorey instance

class WallType(bimascode.ElementType): 
 99class WallType(ElementType):
100    """
101    Wall type defining a compound layer stack.
102
103    A wall type consists of multiple material layers stacked from
104    exterior to interior. The total width is the sum of all layer
105    thicknesses.
106    """
107
108    def __init__(
109        self,
110        name: str,
111        layers: list[Layer] | None = None,
112        description: str | None = None,
113        function: WallFunction = WallFunction.INTERIOR,
114    ):
115        """
116        Create a wall type.
117
118        Args:
119            name: Name for this wall type
120            layers: List of layers from exterior to interior
121            description: Optional description
122            function: Wall function classification (default: INTERIOR)
123        """
124        super().__init__(name)
125        self.layers = layers or []
126        self.description = description
127        self._function = function
128
129        # Store total width as a parameter
130        self._update_width()
131
132    def add_layer(
133        self,
134        material: Material,
135        thickness: Length | float,
136        function: LayerFunction = LayerFunction.OTHER,
137        structural: bool = False,
138        position: int | None = None,
139    ) -> Layer:
140        """
141        Add a material layer to the wall assembly.
142
143        Args:
144            material: Material for this layer
145            thickness: Layer thickness
146            function: Functional role of this layer
147            structural: Whether layer is load-bearing
148            position: Position to insert layer (0=exterior, None=interior)
149
150        Returns:
151            The created Layer object
152        """
153        layer = Layer(material, thickness, function, structural)
154
155        if position is None:
156            self.layers.append(layer)
157        else:
158            self.layers.insert(position, layer)
159
160        self._update_width()
161        return layer
162
163    def remove_layer(self, index: int) -> None:
164        """
165        Remove a layer by index.
166
167        Args:
168            index: Layer index (0 = exterior face)
169        """
170        if 0 <= index < len(self.layers):
171            self.layers.pop(index)
172            self._update_width()
173
174    def get_layer(self, index: int) -> Layer:
175        """
176        Get a layer by index.
177
178        Args:
179            index: Layer index (0 = exterior face)
180
181        Returns:
182            Layer at the specified index
183        """
184        return self.layers[index]
185
186    @property
187    def layer_count(self) -> int:
188        """Get the number of layers."""
189        return len(self.layers)
190
191    @property
192    def total_width(self) -> Length:
193        """Get the total wall width (sum of all layers)."""
194        return Length(sum(layer.thickness_mm for layer in self.layers), "mm")
195
196    @property
197    def total_width_mm(self) -> float:
198        """Get total wall width in millimeters."""
199        return self.total_width.mm
200
201    def _update_width(self) -> None:
202        """Update the width parameter based on layer thicknesses."""
203        self.set_parameter("width", self.total_width_mm)
204
205    def get_structural_layers(self) -> list[Layer]:
206        """Get all structural layers."""
207        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.structural]
208
209    @property
210    def function(self) -> WallFunction:
211        """Get the wall function classification."""
212        return self._function
213
214    @function.setter
215    def function(self, value: WallFunction) -> None:
216        """Set the wall function classification."""
217        self._function = value
218
219    def get_ifc_predefined_type(self) -> str:
220        """
221        Get the IFC predefined type based on wall function.
222
223        Maps WallFunction to appropriate IfcWall predefined types.
224
225        Returns:
226            IFC predefined type string
227        """
228        mapping = {
229            WallFunction.EXTERIOR: "PARTITIONING",
230            WallFunction.INTERIOR: "PARTITIONING",
231            WallFunction.FOUNDATION: "SOLIDWALL",
232            WallFunction.RETAINING: "SOLIDWALL",
233            WallFunction.SOFFIT: "PARTITIONING",
234            WallFunction.CORE_SHAFT: "SHEAR",
235        }
236        return mapping.get(self._function, "NOTDEFINED")
237
238    def get_layers_by_function(self, function: LayerFunction) -> list[Layer]:
239        """
240        Get all layers with a specific function.
241
242        Args:
243            function: Layer function to filter by
244
245        Returns:
246            List of layers with the specified function
247        """
248        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.function == function]
249
250    def create_geometry(self, instance: "Wall") -> Compound:
251        """
252        Create 3D geometry for a wall instance.
253
254        The wall is created as a compound of boxes, one for each layer.
255        Layers are positioned from exterior to interior.
256
257        IMPORTANT: Geometry is created in LOCAL coordinates:
258        - Origin at wall start point
259        - X-axis along wall length direction
260        - Y-axis perpendicular (toward interior)
261        - Z-axis upward
262
263        The IFC placement (in wall.py) handles world positioning.
264
265        Args:
266            instance: Wall instance to create geometry for
267
268        Returns:
269            build123d Compound representing the wall
270        """
271
272        # Get wall parameters
273        start_point = instance.get_parameter("start_point")
274        end_point = instance.get_parameter("end_point")
275        height = instance.get_parameter("height", 3000.0)  # Default 3m
276
277        if start_point is None or end_point is None:
278            raise ValueError("Wall must have start_point and end_point")
279
280        # Calculate wall length
281        import math
282
283        dx = end_point[0] - start_point[0]
284        dy = end_point[1] - start_point[1]
285        length = math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
286
287        # Apply trim adjustments from wall joins
288        # start_offset: negative = trim start back, positive = extend start
289        # end_offset: positive = extend end forward, negative = trim end back
290        start_offset = 0.0
291        end_offset = 0.0
292        if hasattr(instance, "_trim_adjustments") and instance._trim_adjustments:
293            start_offset = instance._trim_adjustments.get("start_offset", 0.0)
294            end_offset = instance._trim_adjustments.get("end_offset", 0.0)
295
296        # Adjusted length accounts for trim/extension at both ends
297        # start_offset: negative = extend start backwards, positive = trim start forward
298        # end_offset: positive = extend end forward, negative = trim end back
299        adjusted_length = length - start_offset + end_offset
300
301        # The geometry origin shifts when start is adjusted
302        # If start_offset is negative (extended backwards), geometry starts at negative X
303        x_origin_offset = start_offset
304
305        # Create layer geometries in LOCAL coordinates
306        # X = along wall length (0 to adjusted_length)
307        # Y = perpendicular to wall (layers stack in Y direction)
308        # Z = height (0 to height)
309        layer_solids = []
310        current_offset = 0.0
311
312        for layer in self.layers:
313            # Create box for this layer using adjusted length
314            layer_box = Box(adjusted_length, layer.thickness_mm, height)
315
316            # Position the layer in local coordinates:
317            # - X: center along wall length, offset by origin adjustment
318            # - Y: centered on wall centerline (Y=0 is centerline)
319            #      Wall extends from Y = -total_width/2 to Y = +total_width/2
320            # - Z: center at half height
321            total_width = sum(lyr.thickness_mm for lyr in self.layers)
322            half_total = total_width / 2.0
323            layer_y_offset = current_offset + layer.thickness_mm / 2 - half_total
324
325            # X position: center of adjusted length, shifted by origin offset
326            x_center = x_origin_offset + adjusted_length / 2
327
328            loc = Location(
329                (x_center, layer_y_offset, height / 2),
330                (0, 0, 1),
331                0,  # No rotation - geometry is in local coords
332            )
333
334            layer_box = layer_box.locate(loc)
335            layer_solids.append(layer_box)
336
337            current_offset += layer.thickness_mm
338
339        # Create wall compound
340        wall_compound = Compound(children=layer_solids)
341
342        # Apply boolean subtraction for hosted element openings
343        if hasattr(instance, "openings") and instance.openings:
344            for opening in instance.openings:
345                try:
346                    wall_compound = wall_compound - opening
347                except Exception:
348                    # Boolean operation failed - skip this opening
349                    # This can happen with edge cases in geometry
350                    pass
351
352        # TODO: 3D miter cuts are disabled pending correct implementation
353        # The miter angles are stored in _trim_adjustments but the 3D boolean
354        # cutting is not working correctly. The 2D representation handles
355        # miter corners correctly via get_plan_representation().
356        #
357        # To re-enable, uncomment the following and fix _create_miter_cutter()
358        # to properly position the cutter on the INSIDE of the L-corner.
359        #
360        # if hasattr(instance, "_trim_adjustments") and instance._trim_adjustments:
361        #     start_miter = instance._trim_adjustments.get("start_miter_angle")
362        #     end_miter = instance._trim_adjustments.get("end_miter_angle")
363        #     ...
364
365        return wall_compound
366
367    def _create_miter_cutter(
368        self,
369        width: float,
370        height: float,
371        length: float,
372        miter_angle: float,
373        x_origin_offset: float,
374        at_start: bool,
375        inside_sign: int = 1,
376    ) -> Compound | None:
377        """
378        Create a triangular prism cutter for miter cutting.
379
380        Directly creates the triangular wedge that needs to be removed from
381        the inside corner, using absolute coordinates.
382
383        Args:
384            width: Wall total width (mm)
385            height: Wall height (mm)
386            length: Adjusted wall length (mm)
387            miter_angle: Angle of miter cut (radians, from perpendicular)
388            x_origin_offset: X offset of wall geometry origin
389            at_start: True to cut start end, False to cut end
390            inside_sign: Which side is inside of L-corner (+1 = +Y side, -1 = -Y side)
391
392        Returns:
393            build123d Compound representing the cutting wedge, or None if invalid
394        """
395        from build123d import Box
396
397        # Prevent division by zero
398        if abs(_math.tan(miter_angle)) < 0.01:
399            return None
400
401        half_width = width / 2
402        cut_depth = half_width / _math.tan(miter_angle)
403        margin = 5
404
405        if at_start:
406            wall_end_x = x_origin_offset
407        else:
408            wall_end_x = x_origin_offset + length
409
410        # The miter cut should create a diagonal from:
411        #   A = (wall_end_x, 0) - centerline at wall end
412        #   B = (wall_end_x ± cut_depth, inside_sign * half_width) - inside edge back
413        #
414        # For wall1's END with inside_sign=-1 (inside on -Y):
415        #   A = (5100, 0)
416        #   B = (5000, -100)
417        #   The diagonal goes from A to B
418        #   Material to remove is a triangle ABX where X is on the inside edge at end
419        #   X = (5100, -100) - inside corner at end
420        #
421        # Triangle to remove (in XY plane, extruded along Z):
422        #   (5100, 0) - (5100, -100) - (5000, -100)
423        #
424        # This is a right triangle with legs along X and Y directions
425
426        # Create a box and position it to cut the triangle area
427        # Since the triangle has one corner at (wall_end_x, 0) and extends:
428        # - Along X by cut_depth (toward start of wall)
429        # - Along Y by half_width (toward inside edge)
430        #
431        # We can use a box rotated 45 degrees positioned at the corner
432
433        # Create a square box larger than the triangle hypotenuse
434        # The hypotenuse length = sqrt(cut_depth^2 + half_width^2)
435        # For 45° angle and equal cut_depth/half_width, this is half_width * sqrt(2)
436        box_size = max(cut_depth, half_width) * 1.5 + margin
437        cutter_box = Box(box_size, box_size, height + margin)
438
439        z_center = height / 2
440        rotation_deg = _math.degrees(miter_angle)
441
442        # Position the box so one corner is at the wall end centerline
443        # and it extends into the inside corner area
444        #
445        # For wall END with inside=-1:
446        # - Box rotated -45° (CW) around its center
447        # - Positioned so the rotated corner touches (5100, 0)
448        # - The box extends into the -Y direction and -X direction
449
450        # The center of a rotated square is offset from its corner by:
451        # corner_offset = box_size / 2 * sqrt(2) at 45° from the rotation direction
452
453        # Instead of complex calculations, position the box so it clearly
454        # covers the triangular region:
455        if at_start:
456            # At start, triangle extends into +X and toward inside
457            dir_x = 1  # Box extends into wall
458            rotation = 45 * inside_sign  # CW for inside=-1, CCW for inside=+1
459        else:
460            # At end, triangle extends into -X and toward inside
461            dir_x = -1
462            rotation = -45 * inside_sign  # CCW for inside=-1, CW for inside=+1
463
464        # Box center at wall end, offset into the inside half and into the wall
465        box_x = wall_end_x + dir_x * box_size / 3
466        box_y = inside_sign * box_size / 3
467
468        cutter_copy = copy.copy(cutter_box)
469
470        # Position and rotate around the box center
471        loc = Location((box_x, box_y, z_center), (0, 0, 1), rotation)
472        cutter_copy = cutter_copy.locate(loc)
473
474        return Compound(children=[cutter_copy])
475
476    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file):
477        """
478        Export wall type to IFC as IfcMaterialLayerSet.
479
480        Args:
481            ifc_file: IFC file object
482
483        Returns:
484            IfcMaterialLayerSet
485        """
486        # Create IFC material layers
487        ifc_layers = []
488        for i, layer in enumerate(self.layers):
489            # Create IFC material for layer
490            ifc_material = layer.material.to_ifc(ifc_file)
491
492            # Create material layer
493            ifc_layer = ifc_file.create_entity(
494                "IfcMaterialLayer",
495                Material=ifc_material,
496                LayerThickness=layer.thickness_mm,
497                IsVentilated=False,
498                Name=layer.description,
499                Category=layer.function.value,
500                Priority=i + 1,
501            )
502            ifc_layers.append(ifc_layer)
503
504        # Create material layer set
505        ifc_layer_set = ifc_file.create_entity(
506            "IfcMaterialLayerSet",
507            MaterialLayers=ifc_layers,
508            LayerSetName=self.name,
509            Description=self.description,
510        )
511
512        return ifc_layer_set
513
514    def __repr__(self) -> str:
515        return f"WallType(name='{self.name}', layers={self.layer_count}, width={self.total_width_mm:.1f}mm)"

Wall type defining a compound layer stack.

A wall type consists of multiple material layers stacked from exterior to interior. The total width is the sum of all layer thicknesses.

WallType( name: str, layers: list[Layer] | None = None, description: str | None = None, function: bimascode.architecture.wall_type.WallFunction = <WallFunction.INTERIOR: 'Interior'>) 
108    def __init__(
109        self,
110        name: str,
111        layers: list[Layer] | None = None,
112        description: str | None = None,
113        function: WallFunction = WallFunction.INTERIOR,
114    ):
115        """
116        Create a wall type.
117
118        Args:
119            name: Name for this wall type
120            layers: List of layers from exterior to interior
121            description: Optional description
122            function: Wall function classification (default: INTERIOR)
123        """
124        super().__init__(name)
125        self.layers = layers or []
126        self.description = description
127        self._function = function
128
129        # Store total width as a parameter
130        self._update_width()

Create a wall type.

Args: name: Name for this wall type layers: List of layers from exterior to interior description: Optional description function: Wall function classification (default: INTERIOR)

layers
description
def add_layer( self, material: bimascode.utils.materials.Material, thickness: Length | float, function: LayerFunction = <LayerFunction.OTHER: 'Other'>, structural: bool = False, position: int | None = None) -> Layer: 
132    def add_layer(
133        self,
134        material: Material,
135        thickness: Length | float,
136        function: LayerFunction = LayerFunction.OTHER,
137        structural: bool = False,
138        position: int | None = None,
139    ) -> Layer:
140        """
141        Add a material layer to the wall assembly.
142
143        Args:
144            material: Material for this layer
145            thickness: Layer thickness
146            function: Functional role of this layer
147            structural: Whether layer is load-bearing
148            position: Position to insert layer (0=exterior, None=interior)
149
150        Returns:
151            The created Layer object
152        """
153        layer = Layer(material, thickness, function, structural)
154
155        if position is None:
156            self.layers.append(layer)
157        else:
158            self.layers.insert(position, layer)
159
160        self._update_width()
161        return layer

Add a material layer to the wall assembly.

Args: material: Material for this layer thickness: Layer thickness function: Functional role of this layer structural: Whether layer is load-bearing position: Position to insert layer (0=exterior, None=interior)

Returns: The created Layer object

def remove_layer(self, index: int) -> None: 
163    def remove_layer(self, index: int) -> None:
164        """
165        Remove a layer by index.
166
167        Args:
168            index: Layer index (0 = exterior face)
169        """
170        if 0 <= index < len(self.layers):
171            self.layers.pop(index)
172            self._update_width()

def get_layer(self, index: int) -> Layer: 
174    def get_layer(self, index: int) -> Layer:
175        """
176        Get a layer by index.
177
178        Args:
179            index: Layer index (0 = exterior face)
180

183        """
184        return self.layers[index]

186    @property

Get total wall width in millimeters.

def get_structural_layers(self) -> list[Layer]: 
205    def get_structural_layers(self) -> list[Layer]:

Get the wall function classification.

def get_ifc_predefined_type(self) -> str: 
219    def get_ifc_predefined_type(self) -> str:
220        """
221        Get the IFC predefined type based on wall function.
222
223        Maps WallFunction to appropriate IfcWall predefined types.
224
225        Returns:
226            IFC predefined type string
227        """
228        mapping = {
229            WallFunction.EXTERIOR: "PARTITIONING",
230            WallFunction.INTERIOR: "PARTITIONING",
231            WallFunction.FOUNDATION: "SOLIDWALL",
232            WallFunction.RETAINING: "SOLIDWALL",
233            WallFunction.SOFFIT: "PARTITIONING",
234            WallFunction.CORE_SHAFT: "SHEAR",
235        }
236        return mapping.get(self._function, "NOTDEFINED")

Get the IFC predefined type based on wall function.
