Returns: IFC predefined type string

def get_layers_by_function( self, function: LayerFunction) -> list[Layer]: 
238    def get_layers_by_function(self, function: LayerFunction) -> list[Layer]:
239        """
240        Get all layers with a specific function.
241
242        Args:
243            function: Layer function to filter by
244
245        Returns:
246            List of layers with the specified function
247        """
248        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.function == function]

Get all layers with a specific function.

Args: function: Layer function to filter by

Returns: List of layers with the specified function

def create_geometry( self, instance: Wall) -> build123d.topology.composite.Compound: 
250    def create_geometry(self, instance: "Wall") -> Compound:
251        """
252        Create 3D geometry for a wall instance.
253
254        The wall is created as a compound of boxes, one for each layer.
255        Layers are positioned from exterior to interior.
256
257        IMPORTANT: Geometry is created in LOCAL coordinates:
258        - Origin at wall start point
259        - X-axis along wall length direction
260        - Y-axis perpendicular (toward interior)
261        - Z-axis upward
262
263        The IFC placement (in wall.py) handles world positioning.
264
265        Args:
266            instance: Wall instance to create geometry for
267
268        Returns:
269            build123d Compound representing the wall
270        """
271
272        # Get wall parameters
273        start_point = instance.get_parameter("start_point")
274        end_point = instance.get_parameter("end_point")
275        height = instance.get_parameter("height", 3000.0)  # Default 3m
276
277        if start_point is None or end_point is None:
278            raise ValueError("Wall must have start_point and end_point")
279
280        # Calculate wall length
281        import math
282
283        dx = end_point[0] - start_point[0]
284        dy = end_point[1] - start_point[1]
285        length = math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
286
287        # Apply trim adjustments from wall joins
288        # start_offset: negative = trim start back, positive = extend start
289        # end_offset: positive = extend end forward, negative = trim end back
290        start_offset = 0.0
291        end_offset = 0.0
292        if hasattr(instance, "_trim_adjustments") and instance._trim_adjustments:
293            start_offset = instance._trim_adjustments.get("start_offset", 0.0)
294            end_offset = instance._trim_adjustments.get("end_offset", 0.0)
295
296        # Adjusted length accounts for trim/extension at both ends
297        # start_offset: negative = extend start backwards, positive = trim start forward
298        # end_offset: positive = extend end forward, negative = trim end back
299        adjusted_length = length - start_offset + end_offset
300
301        # The geometry origin shifts when start is adjusted
302        # If start_offset is negative (extended backwards), geometry starts at negative X
303        x_origin_offset = start_offset
304
305        # Create layer geometries in LOCAL coordinates
306        # X = along wall length (0 to adjusted_length)
307        # Y = perpendicular to wall (layers stack in Y direction)
308        # Z = height (0 to height)
309        layer_solids = []
310        current_offset = 0.0
311
312        for layer in self.layers:
313            # Create box for this layer using adjusted length
314            layer_box = Box(adjusted_length, layer.thickness_mm, height)
315
316            # Position the layer in local coordinates:
317            # - X: center along wall length, offset by origin adjustment
318            # - Y: centered on wall centerline (Y=0 is centerline)
319            #      Wall extends from Y = -total_width/2 to Y = +total_width/2
320            # - Z: center at half height
321            total_width = sum(lyr.thickness_mm for lyr in self.layers)
322            half_total = total_width / 2.0
323            layer_y_offset = current_offset + layer.thickness_mm / 2 - half_total
324
325            # X position: center of adjusted length, shifted by origin offset
326            x_center = x_origin_offset + adjusted_length / 2
327
328            loc = Location(
329                (x_center, layer_y_offset, height / 2),
330                (0, 0, 1),
331                0,  # No rotation - geometry is in local coords
332            )
333
334            layer_box = layer_box.locate(loc)
335            layer_solids.append(layer_box)
336
337            current_offset += layer.thickness_mm
338
339        # Create wall compound
340        wall_compound = Compound(children=layer_solids)
341
342        # Apply boolean subtraction for hosted element openings
343        if hasattr(instance, "openings") and instance.openings:
344            for opening in instance.openings:
345                try:
346                    wall_compound = wall_compound - opening
347                except Exception:
348                    # Boolean operation failed - skip this opening
349                    # This can happen with edge cases in geometry
350                    pass
351
352        # TODO: 3D miter cuts are disabled pending correct implementation
353        # The miter angles are stored in _trim_adjustments but the 3D boolean
354        # cutting is not working correctly. The 2D representation handles
355        # miter corners correctly via get_plan_representation().
356        #
357        # To re-enable, uncomment the following and fix _create_miter_cutter()
358        # to properly position the cutter on the INSIDE of the L-corner.
359        #
360        # if hasattr(instance, "_trim_adjustments") and instance._trim_adjustments:
361        #     start_miter = instance._trim_adjustments.get("start_miter_angle")
362        #     end_miter = instance._trim_adjustments.get("end_miter_angle")
363        #     ...
364
365        return wall_compound

Create 3D geometry for a wall instance.

The wall is created as a compound of boxes, one for each layer. Layers are positioned from exterior to interior.

IMPORTANT: Geometry is created in LOCAL coordinates:

  • Origin at wall start point
  • X-axis along wall length direction
  • Y-axis perpendicular (toward interior)
  • Z-axis upward

The IFC placement (in wall.py) handles world positioning.

Args: instance: Wall instance to create geometry for

Returns: build123d Compound representing the wall

def to_ifc(self, ifc_file): 
476    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file):
477        """
478        Export wall type to IFC as IfcMaterialLayerSet.
479
480        Args:
481            ifc_file: IFC file object
482
483        Returns:
484            IfcMaterialLayerSet
485        """
486        # Create IFC material layers
487        ifc_layers = []
488        for i, layer in enumerate(self.layers):
489            # Create IFC material for layer
490            ifc_material = layer.material.to_ifc(ifc_file)
491
492            # Create material layer
493            ifc_layer = ifc_file.create_entity(
494                "IfcMaterialLayer",
495                Material=ifc_material,
496                LayerThickness=layer.thickness_mm,
497                IsVentilated=False,
498                Name=layer.description,
499                Category=layer.function.value,
500                Priority=i + 1,
501            )
502            ifc_layers.append(ifc_layer)
503
504        # Create material layer set
505        ifc_layer_set = ifc_file.create_entity(
506            "IfcMaterialLayerSet",
507            MaterialLayers=ifc_layers,
508            LayerSetName=self.name,
509            Description=self.description,
510        )
511
512        return ifc_layer_set

Export wall type to IFC as IfcMaterialLayerSet.

Args: ifc_file: IFC file object

Returns: IfcMaterialLayerSet

class Wall(bimascode.ElementInstance, bimascode.core.world_geometry.FreestandingElementMixin): 
 26class Wall(ElementInstance, FreestandingElementMixin):
 27    """
 28    A straight wall element.
 29
 30    Walls are linear elements defined by start and end points.
 31    They belong to a level and extend upward by a specified height.
 32    """
 33
 34    def __init__(
 35        self,
 36        wall_type: "WallType",
 37        start_point: tuple[float, float],
 38        end_point: tuple[float, float],
 39        level: Level,
 40        height: Length | float | None = None,
 41        structural: bool = False,
 42        name: str | None = None,
 43    ):
 44        """
 45        Create a wall.
 46
 47        Args:
 48            wall_type: WallType defining the wall assembly
 49            start_point: (x, y) coordinates of wall start point
 50            end_point: (x, y) coordinates of wall end point
 51            level: Level this wall sits on
 52            height: Wall height (defaults to level height or 3000mm)
 53            structural: If True, wall is structural (shear wall)
 54            name: Optional name for this wall
 55        """
 56        super().__init__(wall_type, name)
 57
 58        self.level = level
 59
 60        # Hosted elements (doors, windows)
 61        self._hosted_elements: list = []
 62
 63        # Wall join trim adjustments
 64        self._trim_adjustments: dict = {}
 65
 66        # Join styles for each end (start=0, end=1)
 67        self._join_style_start: WallJoinStyle = WallJoinStyle.BUTT
 68        self._join_style_end: WallJoinStyle = WallJoinStyle.BUTT
 69
 70        # Structural flag
 71        self._structural = structural
 72
 73        # Store geometric parameters
 74        self.set_parameter("start_point", start_point, override=False)
 75        self.set_parameter("end_point", end_point, override=False)
 76
 77        # Set height (default to 3000mm if not specified)
 78        if height is None:
 79            height = 3000.0
 80
 81        self.set_parameter("height", normalize_length(height).mm, override=False)
 82
 83        # Register with level
 84        if hasattr(level, "add_element"):
 85            level.add_element(self)
 86
 87    @property
 88    def start_point(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
 89        """Get wall start point."""
 90        return self.get_parameter("start_point")
 91
 92    @property
 93    def end_point(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
 94        """Get wall end point."""
 95        return self.get_parameter("end_point")
 96
 97    @property
 98    def height(self) -> float:
 99        """Get wall height in millimeters."""
100        return self.get_parameter("height")
101
102    @property
103    def height_length(self) -> Length:
104        """Get wall height as Length object."""
105        return Length(self.height, "mm")
106
107    @property
108    def length(self) -> float:
109        """Get wall length in millimeters."""
110        start = self.start_point
111        end = self.end_point
112        dx = end[0] - start[0]
113        dy = end[1] - start[1]
114        return math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
115
116    @property
117    def length_length(self) -> Length:
118        """Get wall length as Length object."""
119        return Length(self.length, "mm")
120
121    @property
122    def width(self) -> float:
123        """Get wall width (thickness) in millimeters."""
124        return self.get_parameter("width")
125
126    @property
127    def width_length(self) -> Length:
128        """Get wall width as Length object."""
129        return Length(self.width, "mm")
130
131    @property
132    def angle(self) -> float:
133        """Get wall angle in radians."""
134        start = self.start_point
135        end = self.end_point
136        dx = end[0] - start[0]
137        dy = end[1] - start[1]
138        return math.atan2(dy, dx)
139
140    @property
141    def angle_degrees(self) -> float:
142        """Get wall angle in degrees."""
143        return math.degrees(self.angle)
144
145    def _get_world_position(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
146        """Get world position for wall geometry.
147
148        Returns:
149            (x, y, z) at wall start point, with Z at level elevation
150        """
151        start = self.start_point
152        return (start[0], start[1], self.level.elevation_mm)
153
154    def _get_world_rotation(self) -> float:
155        """Get world rotation for wall geometry.
156
157        Returns:
158            Wall angle in degrees
159        """
160        return self.angle_degrees
161
162    @property
163    def structural(self) -> bool:
164        """Check if wall is marked as structural (shear wall)."""
165        return self._structural
166
167    @structural.setter
168    def structural(self, value: bool) -> None:
169        """Set the structural flag."""
170        self._structural = value
171
172    @property
173    def is_structural(self) -> bool:
174        """Check if wall has structural layers or is marked as structural."""
175        return self._structural or len(self.type.get_structural_layers()) > 0
176
177    @property
178    def join_style_start(self) -> WallJoinStyle:
179        """Get the join style at the wall's start endpoint."""
180        return self._join_style_start
181
182    @join_style_start.setter
183    def join_style_start(self, value: WallJoinStyle) -> None:
184        """Set the join style at the wall's start endpoint."""
185        self._join_style_start = value
186        self.invalidate_geometry()
187
188    @property
189    def join_style_end(self) -> WallJoinStyle:
190        """Get the join style at the wall's end endpoint."""
191        return self._join_style_end
192
193    @join_style_end.setter
194    def join_style_end(self, value: WallJoinStyle) -> None:
195        """Set the join style at the wall's end endpoint."""
196        self._join_style_end = value
197        self.invalidate_geometry()
198
199    def set_join_style(self, end: int, style: WallJoinStyle) -> None:
200        """
201        Set the join style for a specific endpoint.
202
203        Args:
204            end: 0 for start endpoint, 1 for end endpoint
205            style: Join style to apply
206        """
207        if end == 0:
208            self.join_style_start = style
209        elif end == 1:
210            self.join_style_end = style
211        else:
212            raise ValueError("end must be 0 (start) or 1 (end)")
213
214    def get_join_style(self, end: int) -> WallJoinStyle:
215        """
216        Get the join style for a specific endpoint.
217
218        Args:
219            end: 0 for start endpoint, 1 for end endpoint
220
221        Returns:
222            Join style at the specified endpoint
223        """
224        if end == 0:
225            return self._join_style_start
226        elif end == 1:
227            return self._join_style_end
228        else:
229            raise ValueError("end must be 0 (start) or 1 (end)")
230
231    @property
232    def hosted_elements(self) -> list:
233        """Get all hosted elements (doors, windows)."""
234        return self._hosted_elements.copy()
235
236    @property
237    def openings(self) -> list:
238        """Get opening geometries from hosted elements."""
239        opening_geoms = []
240        for element in self._hosted_elements:
241            if hasattr(element, "get_opening_geometry"):
242                opening_geoms.append(element.get_opening_geometry())
243        return opening_geoms
244
245    def add_hosted_element(self, element) -> None:
246        """
247        Add a hosted element (door or window) to this wall.
248
249        Args:
250            element: Door or Window to host
251        """
252        if element not in self._hosted_elements:
253            self._hosted_elements.append(element)
254            self.invalidate_geometry()
255
256    def remove_hosted_element(self, element) -> None:
257        """
258        Remove a hosted element from this wall.
259
260        Args:
261            element: Door or Window to remove
262        """
263        if element in self._hosted_elements:
264            self._hosted_elements.remove(element)
265            self.invalidate_geometry()
266
267    def get_midpoint(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
268        """
269        Get the midpoint of the wall.
270
271        Returns:
272            (x, y) coordinates of wall centerpoint
273        """
274        start = self.start_point
275        end = self.end_point
276        return ((start[0] + end[0]) / 2, (start[1] + end[1]) / 2)
277
278    def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
279        """
280        Get the 3D center point of the wall.
281
282        Returns:
283            (x, y, z) coordinates of wall center
284        """
285        mid_x, mid_y = self.get_midpoint()
286        z = self.level.elevation_mm + self.height / 2
287        return (mid_x, mid_y, z)
288
289    def reverse(self) -> None:
290        """Reverse the wall direction (swap start and end points)."""
291        start = self.start_point
292        end = self.end_point
293        self.set_parameter("start_point", end, override=False)
294        self.set_parameter("end_point", start, override=False)
295        self.invalidate_geometry()
296
297    def set_start_point(self, point: tuple[float, float]) -> None:
298        """
299        Set the wall start point.
300
301        Args:
302            point: (x, y) coordinates
303        """
304        self.set_parameter("start_point", point, override=False)
305        self.invalidate_geometry()
306
307    def set_end_point(self, point: tuple[float, float]) -> None:
308        """
309        Set the wall end point.
310
311        Args:
312            point: (x, y) coordinates
313        """
314        self.set_parameter("end_point", point, override=False)
315        self.invalidate_geometry()
316
317    def set_height(self, height: Length | float) -> None:
318        """
319        Set the wall height.
320
321        Args:
322            height: New wall height
323        """
324        self.set_parameter("height", normalize_length(height).mm, override=False)
325        self.invalidate_geometry()
326
327    def set_trim_adjustments(self, adjustments: dict) -> None:
328        """
329        Set wall join trim adjustments.
330
331        Trim adjustments control how the wall geometry is extended or trimmed
332        at joins with other walls.
333
334        Args:
335            adjustments: Dict with 'start_offset' and 'end_offset' values (mm).
336                        Negative start_offset extends the start backward.
337                        Positive end_offset extends the end forward.
338        """
339        self._trim_adjustments = adjustments
340        self.invalidate_geometry()
341
342    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey):
343        """
344        Export wall to IFC.
345
346        Args:
347            ifc_file: IFC file object
348            ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place wall in
349
350        Returns:
351            IfcWall entity
352        """
353        # Determine predefined type: structural flag overrides, otherwise use wall function
354        if self._structural:
355            predefined_type = "SHEAR"
356        else:
357            predefined_type = self.type.get_ifc_predefined_type()
358
359        # Create wall
360        ifc_wall = ifc_file.create_entity(
361            "IfcWall",
362            GlobalId=self.guid,
363            Name=self.name,
364            Description=f"{self.type.name} wall",
365            PredefinedType=predefined_type,
366        )
367
368        # Set placement (local to building storey)
369        start = self.start_point
370        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((float(start[0]), float(start[1]), 0.0))
371
372        axis_placement = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(
373            location,
374            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0)),
375            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((math.cos(self.angle), math.sin(self.angle), 0.0)),
376        )
377
378        local_placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
379            ifc_building_storey.ObjectPlacement, axis_placement
380        )
381
382        ifc_wall.ObjectPlacement = local_placement
383
384        # Create geometry representation using BREP
385        geom = self.get_geometry()
386
387        if geom:
388            from bimascode.export.ifc_geometry import build123d_to_ifc_brep
389
390            # Convert build123d geometry to IFC BREP
391            ifc_brep = build123d_to_ifc_brep(geom, ifc_file)
392
393            if ifc_brep:
394                # Create shape representation with BREP
395                shape_representation = ifc_file.createIfcShapeRepresentation(
396                    ifc_file.by_type("IfcGeometricRepresentationContext")[0],
397                    "Body",
398                    "Brep",
399                    [ifc_brep],
400                )
401
402                # Create product definition shape
403                product_shape = ifc_file.createIfcProductDefinitionShape(
404                    None, None, [shape_representation]
405                )
406
407                ifc_wall.Representation = product_shape
408
409        # Associate with building storey
410        ifc_file.createIfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure(
411            self._generate_guid(),
412            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
413            f"Wall{self.name}Container",
414            None,
415            [ifc_wall],
416            ifc_building_storey,
417        )
418
419        # Associate with material layer set
420        material_layer_set = self.type.to_ifc(ifc_file)
421
422        # Create material layer set usage
423        material_layer_set_usage = ifc_file.create_entity(
424            "IfcMaterialLayerSetUsage",
425            ForLayerSet=material_layer_set,
426            LayerSetDirection="AXIS2",
427            DirectionSense="POSITIVE",
428            OffsetFromReferenceLine=0.0,
429        )
430
431        ifc_file.createIfcRelAssociatesMaterial(
432            self._generate_guid(),
433            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
434            None,
435            None,
436            [ifc_wall],
437            material_layer_set_usage,
438        )
439
440        return ifc_wall
441
442    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox:
443        """Get axis-aligned bounding box for this wall.
444
445        Returns:
446            BoundingBox encompassing the wall geometry
447        """
448        start = self.start_point
449        end = self.end_point
450        width = self.width
451        height = self.height
452
453        # Calculate perpendicular offset for wall thickness
454        half_width = width / 2.0
455
456        # Get min/max from start and end points
457        min_x = min(start[0], end[0]) - half_width
458        max_x = max(start[0], end[0]) + half_width
459        min_y = min(start[1], end[1]) - half_width
460        max_y = max(start[1], end[1]) + half_width
461
462        # Z coordinates from level elevation
463        min_z = self.level.elevation_mm
464        max_z = min_z + height
465
466        return BoundingBox(min_x, min_y, min_z, max_x, max_y, max_z)
467
468    def get_plan_representation(
469        self,
470        cut_height: float,
471        view_range: "ViewRange",
472        symbology: "ElementSymbology | None" = None,
473    ) -> list[Union["Line2D", "Arc2D", "Polyline2D", "Hatch2D"]]:
474        """Generate floor plan linework for this wall.
475
476        Creates polylines representing the wall outline, accounting for:
477        - Wall join trim adjustments at corners
478        - Openings from hosted doors and windows
479
480        Args:
481            cut_height: Z coordinate of the section cut
482            view_range: View range parameters
483            symbology: Optional symbology settings (None uses AIA defaults)
484
485        Returns:
486            List of 2D geometry primitives
487        """
488        from bimascode.drawing.hatch_patterns import get_hatch_pattern_for_layer
489        from bimascode.drawing.line_styles import Layer, LineStyle
490        from bimascode.drawing.primitives import Hatch2D, Point2D, Polyline2D
491        from bimascode.drawing.symbology import FillMode, get_default_symbology
492
493        # Use provided symbology or get default
494        if symbology is None:
495            symbology = get_default_symbology("Wall")
496
497        # Check if wall is cut by the section plane
498        bbox = self.get_bounding_box()
499        is_cut = bbox.min_z <= cut_height <= bbox.max_z
500
501        if not is_cut:
502            style = symbology.visible_style or LineStyle.visible()
503        else:
504            style = symbology.cut_style or LineStyle.cut_heavy()
505
506        # Wall geometry
507        start = self.start_point
508        end = self.end_point
509        half_width = self.width / 2.0
510        angle = self.angle
511        wall_length = self.length
512
513        # Direction vectors
514        cos_a = math.cos(angle)
515        sin_a = math.sin(angle)
516
517        # Perpendicular offset vectors (left side is positive)
518        perp_x = -sin_a * half_width
519        perp_y = cos_a * half_width
520
521        # Apply trim adjustments from wall joins
522        # start_offset: negative = extend start backwards, positive = trim start forward
523        # end_offset: positive = extend end forward, negative = trim end back
524        start_offset = 0.0
525        end_offset = 0.0
526        if hasattr(self, "_trim_adjustments") and self._trim_adjustments:
527            start_offset = self._trim_adjustments.get("start_offset", 0.0)
528            end_offset = self._trim_adjustments.get("end_offset", 0.0)
529
530        # Adjusted start and end points along centerline
531        adj_start_x = start[0] + start_offset * cos_a
532        adj_start_y = start[1] + start_offset * sin_a
533        adj_end_x = end[0] + end_offset * cos_a
534        adj_end_y = end[1] + end_offset * sin_a
535
536        # Get miter angles and inside signs for diagonal corner cuts
537        start_miter = self._trim_adjustments.get("start_miter_angle") if self._trim_adjustments else None
538        end_miter = self._trim_adjustments.get("end_miter_angle") if self._trim_adjustments else None
539        start_inside_sign = self._trim_adjustments.get("start_miter_inside_sign", 1) if self._trim_adjustments else 1
540        end_inside_sign = self._trim_adjustments.get("end_miter_inside_sign", 1) if self._trim_adjustments else 1
541
542        # Calculate miter corner offsets for diagonal cuts at L-corners.
543        # The inside corner cuts back, the outside corner extends forward.
544        # This creates a diagonal line on the inside of the L-corner.
545        #
546        # inside_sign indicates which side is inside the L-corner:
547        #   +1 = left side (+perp)
548        #   -1 = right side (-perp)
549
550        # Start miter offsets (positive offset = toward wall end)
551        start_left_offset = 0.0
552        start_right_offset = 0.0
553        if start_miter is not None and abs(start_miter) > 0.01:
554            miter_ext = half_width / math.tan(start_miter)
555            if start_inside_sign > 0:
556                # Inside on left: left cuts back (toward end), right extends back
557                start_left_offset = miter_ext
558                start_right_offset = -miter_ext
559            else:
560                # Inside on right: right cuts back, left extends back
561                start_left_offset = -miter_ext
562                start_right_offset = miter_ext
563
564        # End miter offsets (positive offset = past wall end)
565        end_left_offset = 0.0
566        end_right_offset = 0.0
567        if end_miter is not None and abs(end_miter) > 0.01:
568            miter_ext = half_width / math.tan(end_miter)
569            if end_inside_sign > 0:
570                # Inside on left: left cuts back, right extends forward
571                end_left_offset = -miter_ext
572                end_right_offset = miter_ext
573            else:
574                # Inside on right: right cuts back, left extends forward
575                end_left_offset = miter_ext
576                end_right_offset = -miter_ext
577
578        # Collect openings (doors/windows) that are cut by the section plane
579        openings = []
580        for element in self._hosted_elements:
581            elem_bbox = element.get_bounding_box() if hasattr(element, "get_bounding_box") else None
582            if elem_bbox and elem_bbox.min_z <= cut_height <= elem_bbox.max_z:
583                # Opening is cut - get its position along wall
584                offset = element.offset if hasattr(element, "offset") else 0
585                width = element.width if hasattr(element, "width") else 0
586                openings.append((offset, offset + width))
587
588        # Sort openings by start position
589        openings.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
590
591        result: list[Line2D | Arc2D | Polyline2D | Hatch2D] = []
592
593        # If no openings, draw wall as single polyline
594        if not openings:
595            # Apply miter offsets to corners
596            # Left edge uses +perp, right edge uses -perp
597            corners = [
598                # Start left corner (+perp side)
599                Point2D(
600                    adj_start_x + perp_x + start_left_offset * cos_a,
601                    adj_start_y + perp_y + start_left_offset * sin_a,
602                ),
603                # End left corner (+perp side)
604                Point2D(
605                    adj_end_x + perp_x + end_left_offset * cos_a,
606                    adj_end_y + perp_y + end_left_offset * sin_a,
607                ),
608                # End right corner (-perp side)
609                Point2D(
610                    adj_end_x - perp_x + end_right_offset * cos_a,
611                    adj_end_y - perp_y + end_right_offset * sin_a,
612                ),
613                # Start right corner (-perp side)
614                Point2D(
615                    adj_start_x - perp_x + start_right_offset * cos_a,
616                    adj_start_y - perp_y + start_right_offset * sin_a,
617                ),
618            ]
619            wall_outline = Polyline2D(
620                points=corners,
621                closed=True,
622                style=style,
623                layer=Layer.WALL,
624            )
625            result.append(wall_outline)
626            # Add fill for cut walls based on symbology
627            if is_cut:
628                if symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.SOLID:
629                    # Solid fill hatch using wall outline
630                    solid_hatch = Hatch2D(
631                        boundary=corners,
632                        pattern=None,  # Solid fill
633                        layer=Layer.WALL,
634                        color=symbology.fill_color or (0, 0, 0),
635                    )
636                    result.append(solid_hatch)
637                elif symbology.show_hatching and symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.MATERIAL:
638                    result.extend(
639                        self._generate_layer_hatches(
640                            adj_start_x,
641                            adj_start_y,
642                            adj_end_x,
643                            adj_end_y,
644                            cos_a,
645                            sin_a,
646                            half_width,
647                            get_hatch_pattern_for_layer,
648                        )
649                    )
650        else:
651            # Draw wall segments between openings
652            # Build list of solid segments: (start_offset, end_offset)
653            segments = []
654            current_pos = 0.0
655
656            for open_start, open_end in openings:
657                if open_start > current_pos:
658                    segments.append((current_pos, open_start))
659                current_pos = max(current_pos, open_end)
660
661            # Add final segment after last opening
662            if current_pos < wall_length:
663                segments.append((current_pos, wall_length))
664
665            # Draw each segment as a closed polyline
666            for seg_start, seg_end in segments:
667                # Account for trim adjustments at wall ends
668                actual_start = seg_start
669                actual_end = seg_end
670
671                if seg_start == 0:
672                    actual_start = start_offset  # May be negative (extension)
673                if seg_end == wall_length:
674                    actual_end = wall_length + end_offset
675
676                # Segment corners
677                s_x = start[0] + actual_start * cos_a
678                s_y = start[1] + actual_start * sin_a
679                e_x = start[0] + actual_end * cos_a
680                e_y = start[1] + actual_end * sin_a
681
682                # Apply miter offsets only at actual wall ends (not at openings)
683                seg_start_left = start_left_offset if seg_start == 0 else 0.0
684                seg_start_right = start_right_offset if seg_start == 0 else 0.0
685                seg_end_left = end_left_offset if seg_end == wall_length else 0.0
686                seg_end_right = end_right_offset if seg_end == wall_length else 0.0
687
688                corners = [
689                    Point2D(
690                        s_x + perp_x + seg_start_left * cos_a,
691                        s_y + perp_y + seg_start_left * sin_a,
692                    ),
693                    Point2D(
694                        e_x + perp_x + seg_end_left * cos_a,
695                        e_y + perp_y + seg_end_left * sin_a,
696                    ),
697                    Point2D(
698                        e_x - perp_x + seg_end_right * cos_a,
699                        e_y - perp_y + seg_end_right * sin_a,
700                    ),
701                    Point2D(
702                        s_x - perp_x + seg_start_right * cos_a,
703                        s_y - perp_y + seg_start_right * sin_a,
704                    ),
705                ]
706                seg_outline = Polyline2D(
707                    points=corners,
708                    closed=True,
709                    style=style,
710                    layer=Layer.WALL,
711                )
712                result.append(seg_outline)
713                # Add fill for cut wall segments based on symbology
714                if is_cut:
715                    if symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.SOLID:
716                        # Solid fill hatch using segment outline
717                        solid_hatch = Hatch2D(
718                            boundary=corners,
719                            pattern=None,  # Solid fill
720                            layer=Layer.WALL,
721                            color=symbology.fill_color or (0, 0, 0),
722                        )
723                        result.append(solid_hatch)
724                    elif symbology.show_hatching and symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.MATERIAL:
725                        result.extend(
726                            self._generate_layer_hatches(
727                                s_x,
728                                s_y,
729                                e_x,
730                                e_y,
731                                cos_a,
732                                sin_a,
733                                half_width,
734                                get_hatch_pattern_for_layer,
735                            )
736                        )
737
738        return result
739
740    def _generate_layer_hatches(
741        self,
742        start_x: float,
743        start_y: float,
744        end_x: float,
745        end_y: float,
746        cos_a: float,
747        sin_a: float,
748        half_width: float,
749        get_hatch_pattern_for_layer,
750    ) -> list:
751        """Generate per-layer hatches for a wall segment.
752
753        Creates one hatch per material layer, positioned from exterior to interior.
754
755        Args:
756            start_x, start_y: Segment start point on centerline
757            end_x, end_y: Segment end point on centerline
758            cos_a, sin_a: Direction cosines for wall angle
759            half_width: Half of total wall width
760            get_hatch_pattern_for_layer: Function to get hatch pattern for a layer
761
762        Returns:
763            List of Hatch2D objects, one per layer
764        """
765        from bimascode.drawing.line_styles import Layer
766        from bimascode.drawing.primitives import Hatch2D, Point2D
767
768        hatches = []
769
770        # Current offset from centerline, starting at exterior edge
771        current_offset = -half_width
772
773        for layer in self.type.layers:
774            layer_thickness = layer.thickness_mm
775
776            # Calculate perpendicular offsets for this layer's edges
777            # Exterior edge of this layer
778            ext_offset = current_offset
779            # Interior edge of this layer
780            int_offset = current_offset + layer_thickness
781
782            # Perpendicular vectors for each edge
783            ext_perp_x = -sin_a * ext_offset
784            ext_perp_y = cos_a * ext_offset
785            int_perp_x = -sin_a * int_offset
786            int_perp_y = cos_a * int_offset
787
788            # Layer boundary corners (exterior edge first, then interior)
789            layer_corners = [
790                Point2D(start_x - ext_perp_x, start_y - ext_perp_y),
791                Point2D(end_x - ext_perp_x, end_y - ext_perp_y),
792                Point2D(end_x - int_perp_x, end_y - int_perp_y),
793                Point2D(start_x - int_perp_x, start_y - int_perp_y),
794            ]
795
796            # Get material-based hatch pattern
797            pattern = get_hatch_pattern_for_layer(layer)
798
799            hatch = Hatch2D(
800                boundary=layer_corners,
801                pattern=pattern.name,
802                scale=pattern.scale,
803                rotation=pattern.rotation,
804                color=pattern.color,
805                layer=Layer.WALL,
806            )
807            hatches.append(hatch)
808
809            # Move to next layer
810            current_offset += layer_thickness
811
812        return hatches
813
814    def __repr__(self) -> str:
815        return (
816            f"Wall(name='{self.name}', type='{self.type.name}', "
817            f"length={self.length:.1f}mm, height={self.height:.1f}mm)"
818        )

A straight wall element.

Walls are linear elements defined by start and end points. They belong to a level and extend upward by a specified height.

Wall( wall_type: WallType, start_point: tuple[float, float], end_point: tuple[float, float], level: Level, height: Length | float | None = None, structural: bool = False, name: str | None = None) 
34    def __init__(
35        self,
36        wall_type: "WallType",
37        start_point: tuple[float, float],
38        end_point: tuple[float, float],
39        level: Level,
40        height: Length | float | None = None,
41        structural: bool = False,
42        name: str | None = None,
43    ):
44        """
45        Create a wall.
46
47        Args:
48            wall_type: WallType defining the wall assembly
49            start_point: (x, y) coordinates of wall start point
50            end_point: (x, y) coordinates of wall end point
51            level: Level this wall sits on
52            height: Wall height (defaults to level height or 3000mm)
53            structural: If True, wall is structural (shear wall)
54            name: Optional name for this wall
55        """
56        super().__init__(wall_type, name)
57
58        self.level = level
59
60        # Hosted elements (doors, windows)
61        self._hosted_elements: list = []
62
63        # Wall join trim adjustments
64        self._trim_adjustments: dict = {}
65
66        # Join styles for each end (start=0, end=1)
67        self._join_style_start: WallJoinStyle = WallJoinStyle.BUTT
68        self._join_style_end: WallJoinStyle = WallJoinStyle.BUTT
69
70        # Structural flag
71        self._structural = structural
72
73        # Store geometric parameters
74        self.set_parameter("start_point", start_point, override=False)
75        self.set_parameter("end_point", end_point, override=False)
76
77        # Set height (default to 3000mm if not specified)
78        if height is None:
79            height = 3000.0
80
81        self.set_parameter("height", normalize_length(height).mm, override=False)
82
83        # Register with level
84        if hasattr(level, "add_element"):
85            level.add_element(self)

Create a wall.

Args: wall_type: WallType defining the wall assembly start_point: (x, y) coordinates of wall start point end_point: (x, y) coordinates of wall end point level: Level this wall sits on height: Wall height (defaults to level height or 3000mm) structural: If True, wall is structural (shear wall) name: Optional name for this wall

level
start_point: tuple[float, float] 
87    @property
88    def start_point(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
89        """Get wall start point."""
90        return self.get_parameter("start_point")

Get wall start point.

end_point: tuple[float, float] 
92    @property
93    def end_point(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
94        """Get wall end point."""
95        return self.get_parameter("end_point")

Get wall end point.

height: float 
 97    @property
 98    def height(self) -> float:
 99        """Get wall height in millimeters."""
100        return self.get_parameter("height")

Get wall height in millimeters.

height_length: Length 
102    @property
103    def height_length(self) -> Length:
104        """Get wall height as Length object."""
105        return Length(self.height, "mm")

Get wall height as Length object.

length: float 
107    @property
108    def length(self) -> float:
109        """Get wall length in millimeters."""
110        start = self.start_point
111        end = self.end_point
112        dx = end[0] - start[0]
113        dy = end[1] - start[1]
114        return math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)

Get wall length in millimeters.

length_length: Length 
116    @property
117    def length_length(self) -> Length:
118        """Get wall length as Length object."""
119        return Length(self.length, "mm")

Get wall length as Length object.

width: float 
121    @property
122    def width(self) -> float:
123        """Get wall width (thickness) in millimeters."""
124        return self.get_parameter("width")

Get wall width (thickness) in millimeters.

width_length: Length 
126    @property
127    def width_length(self) -> Length:
128        """Get wall width as Length object."""
129        return Length(self.width, "mm")

Get wall width as Length object.

angle: float 
131    @property
132    def angle(self) -> float:
133        """Get wall angle in radians."""
134        start = self.start_point
135        end = self.end_point
136        dx = end[0] - start[0]
137        dy = end[1] - start[1]
138        return math.atan2(dy, dx)

Get wall angle in radians.

angle_degrees: float 
140    @property
141    def angle_degrees(self) -> float:
142        """Get wall angle in degrees."""
143        return math.degrees(self.angle)

Get wall angle in degrees.

structural: bool 
162    @property
163    def structural(self) -> bool:
164        """Check if wall is marked as structural (shear wall)."""
165        return self._structural

Check if wall is marked as structural (shear wall).

is_structural: bool 
172    @property
173    def is_structural(self) -> bool:
174        """Check if wall has structural layers or is marked as structural."""
175        return self._structural or len(self.type.get_structural_layers()) > 0

Check if wall has structural layers or is marked as structural.

join_style_start: bimascode.architecture.wall_joins.WallJoinStyle 
177    @property
178    def join_style_start(self) -> WallJoinStyle:
179        """Get the join style at the wall's start endpoint."""
180        return self._join_style_start

Get the join style at the wall's start endpoint.

join_style_end: bimascode.architecture.wall_joins.WallJoinStyle 
188    @property
189    def join_style_end(self) -> WallJoinStyle:
190        """Get the join style at the wall's end endpoint."""
191        return self._join_style_end

Get the join style at the wall's end endpoint.

def set_join_style( self, end: int, style: bimascode.architecture.wall_joins.WallJoinStyle) -> None: 
199    def set_join_style(self, end: int, style: WallJoinStyle) -> None:
200        """
201        Set the join style for a specific endpoint.
202
203        Args:
204            end: 0 for start endpoint, 1 for end endpoint
205            style: Join style to apply
206        """
207        if end == 0:
208            self.join_style_start = style
209        elif end == 1:
210            self.join_style_end = style
211        else:
212            raise ValueError("end must be 0 (start) or 1 (end)")

Set the join style for a specific endpoint.

Args: end: 0 for start endpoint, 1 for end endpoint style: Join style to apply

def get_join_style(self, end: int) -> bimascode.architecture.wall_joins.WallJoinStyle: 
214    def get_join_style(self, end: int) -> WallJoinStyle:
215        """
216        Get the join style for a specific endpoint.
217
218        Args:
219            end: 0 for start endpoint, 1 for end endpoint
220
221        Returns:
222            Join style at the specified endpoint
223        """
224        if end == 0:
225            return self._join_style_start
226        elif end == 1:
227            return self._join_style_end
228        else:
229            raise ValueError("end must be 0 (start) or 1 (end)")

Get the join style for a specific endpoint.

Args: end: 0 for start endpoint, 1 for end endpoint

Returns: Join style at the specified endpoint

hosted_elements: list 
231    @property
232    def hosted_elements(self) -> list:
233        """Get all hosted elements (doors, windows)."""
234        return self._hosted_elements.copy()

Get all hosted elements (doors, windows).

openings: list 
236    @property
237    def openings(self) -> list:
238        """Get opening geometries from hosted elements."""
239        opening_geoms = []
240        for element in self._hosted_elements:
241            if hasattr(element, "get_opening_geometry"):
242                opening_geoms.append(element.get_opening_geometry())
243        return opening_geoms

Get opening geometries from hosted elements.

def add_hosted_element(self, element) -> None: 
245    def add_hosted_element(self, element) -> None:
246        """
247        Add a hosted element (door or window) to this wall.
248
249        Args:
250            element: Door or Window to host
251        """
252        if element not in self._hosted_elements:
253            self._hosted_elements.append(element)
254            self.invalidate_geometry()

Add a hosted element (door or window) to this wall.

Args: element: Door or Window to host

def remove_hosted_element(self, element) -> None: 
256    def remove_hosted_element(self, element) -> None:
257        """
258        Remove a hosted element from this wall.
259
260        Args:
261            element: Door or Window to remove
262        """
263        if element in self._hosted_elements:
264            self._hosted_elements.remove(element)
265            self.invalidate_geometry()

Remove a hosted element from this wall.

Args: element: Door or Window to remove

def get_midpoint(self) -> tuple[float, float]: 
267    def get_midpoint(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
268        """
269        Get the midpoint of the wall.
270
271        Returns:
272            (x, y) coordinates of wall centerpoint
273        """
274        start = self.start_point
275        end = self.end_point
276        return ((start[0] + end[0]) / 2, (start[1] + end[1]) / 2)

Get the midpoint of the wall.

Returns: (x, y) coordinates of wall centerpoint

def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]: 
278    def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
279        """
280        Get the 3D center point of the wall.
281
282        Returns:
283            (x, y, z) coordinates of wall center
284        """
285        mid_x, mid_y = self.get_midpoint()
286        z = self.level.elevation_mm + self.height / 2
287        return (mid_x, mid_y, z)

Get the 3D center point of the wall.

Returns: (x, y, z) coordinates of wall center

def reverse(self) -> None: 
289    def reverse(self) -> None:
290        """Reverse the wall direction (swap start and end points)."""
291        start = self.start_point
292        end = self.end_point
293        self.set_parameter("start_point", end, override=False)
294        self.set_parameter("end_point", start, override=False)
295        self.invalidate_geometry()

Reverse the wall direction (swap start and end points).

def set_start_point(self, point: tuple[float, float]) -> None: 
297    def set_start_point(self, point: tuple[float, float]) -> None:
298        """
299        Set the wall start point.
300
301        Args:
302            point: (x, y) coordinates
303        """
304        self.set_parameter("start_point", point, override=False)
305        self.invalidate_geometry()

Set the wall start point.

Args: point: (x, y) coordinates

def set_end_point(self, point: tuple[float, float]) -> None: 
307    def set_end_point(self, point: tuple[float, float]) -> None:
308        """
309        Set the wall end point.
310
311        Args:
312            point: (x, y) coordinates
313        """
314        self.set_parameter("end_point", point, override=False)
315        self.invalidate_geometry()

Set the wall end point.

Args: point: (x, y) coordinates

def set_height(self, height: Length | float) -> None: 
317    def set_height(self, height: Length | float) -> None:
318        """
319        Set the wall height.
320
321        Args:
322            height: New wall height
323        """
324        self.set_parameter("height", normalize_length(height).mm, override=False)
325        self.invalidate_geometry()

Set the wall height.

Args: height: New wall height

def set_trim_adjustments(self, adjustments: dict) -> None: 
327    def set_trim_adjustments(self, adjustments: dict) -> None:
328        """
329        Set wall join trim adjustments.
330
331        Trim adjustments control how the wall geometry is extended or trimmed
332        at joins with other walls.
333
334        Args:
335            adjustments: Dict with 'start_offset' and 'end_offset' values (mm).
336                        Negative start_offset extends the start backward.
337                        Positive end_offset extends the end forward.
338        """
339        self._trim_adjustments = adjustments
340        self.invalidate_geometry()

Set wall join trim adjustments.

Trim adjustments control how the wall geometry is extended or trimmed at joins with other walls.

Args: adjustments: Dict with 'start_offset' and 'end_offset' values (mm). Negative start_offset extends the start backward. Positive end_offset extends the end forward.

def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey): 
342    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey):
343        """
344        Export wall to IFC.
345
346        Args:
347            ifc_file: IFC file object
348            ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place wall in
349
350        Returns:
351            IfcWall entity
352        """
353        # Determine predefined type: structural flag overrides, otherwise use wall function
354        if self._structural:
355            predefined_type = "SHEAR"
356        else:
357            predefined_type = self.type.get_ifc_predefined_type()
358
359        # Create wall
360        ifc_wall = ifc_file.create_entity(
361            "IfcWall",
362            GlobalId=self.guid,
363            Name=self.name,
364            Description=f"{self.type.name} wall",
365            PredefinedType=predefined_type,
366        )
367
368        # Set placement (local to building storey)
369        start = self.start_point
370        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((float(start[0]), float(start[1]), 0.0))
371
372        axis_placement = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(
373            location,
374            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0)),
375            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((math.cos(self.angle), math.sin(self.angle), 0.0)),
376        )
377
378        local_placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
379            ifc_building_storey.ObjectPlacement, axis_placement
380        )
381
382        ifc_wall.ObjectPlacement = local_placement
383
384        # Create geometry representation using BREP
385        geom = self.get_geometry()
386
387        if geom:
388            from bimascode.export.ifc_geometry import build123d_to_ifc_brep
389
390            # Convert build123d geometry to IFC BREP
391            ifc_brep = build123d_to_ifc_brep(geom, ifc_file)
392
393            if ifc_brep:
394                # Create shape representation with BREP
395                shape_representation = ifc_file.createIfcShapeRepresentation(
396                    ifc_file.by_type("IfcGeometricRepresentationContext")[0],
397                    "Body",
398                    "Brep",
399                    [ifc_brep],
400                )
401
402                # Create product definition shape
403                product_shape = ifc_file.createIfcProductDefinitionShape(
404                    None, None, [shape_representation]
405                )
406
407                ifc_wall.Representation = product_shape
408
409        # Associate with building storey
410        ifc_file.createIfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure(
411            self._generate_guid(),
412            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
413            f"Wall{self.name}Container",
414            None,
415            [ifc_wall],
416            ifc_building_storey,
417        )
418
419        # Associate with material layer set
420        material_layer_set = self.type.to_ifc(ifc_file)
421
422        # Create material layer set usage
423        material_layer_set_usage = ifc_file.create_entity(
424            "IfcMaterialLayerSetUsage",
425            ForLayerSet=material_layer_set,
426            LayerSetDirection="AXIS2",
427            DirectionSense="POSITIVE",
428            OffsetFromReferenceLine=0.0,
429        )
430
431        ifc_file.createIfcRelAssociatesMaterial(
432            self._generate_guid(),
433            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
434            None,
435            None,
436            [ifc_wall],
437            material_layer_set_usage,
438        )
439
440        return ifc_wall

Export wall to IFC.

Args: ifc_file: IFC file object ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place wall in

Returns: IfcWall entity

def get_bounding_box(self) -> bimascode.performance.bounding_box.BoundingBox: 
442    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox:
443        """Get axis-aligned bounding box for this wall.
444
445        Returns:
446            BoundingBox encompassing the wall geometry
447        """
448        start = self.start_point
449        end = self.end_point
450        width = self.width
451        height = self.height
452
453        # Calculate perpendicular offset for wall thickness
454        half_width = width / 2.0
455
456        # Get min/max from start and end points
457        min_x = min(start[0], end[0]) - half_width
458        max_x = max(start[0], end[0]) + half_width
459        min_y = min(start[1], end[1]) - half_width
460        max_y = max(start[1], end[1]) + half_width
461
462        # Z coordinates from level elevation
463        min_z = self.level.elevation_mm
464        max_z = min_z + height
465
466        return BoundingBox(min_x, min_y, min_z, max_x, max_y, max_z)

Get axis-aligned bounding box for this wall.

Returns: BoundingBox encompassing the wall geometry

def get_plan_representation( self, cut_height: float, view_range: bimascode.drawing.view_base.ViewRange, symbology: bimascode.drawing.symbology.ElementSymbology | None = None) -> list[typing.Union[bimascode.drawing.primitives.Line2D, bimascode.drawing.primitives.Arc2D, bimascode.drawing.primitives.Polyline2D, bimascode.drawing.primitives.Hatch2D]]: 
468    def get_plan_representation(
469        self,
470        cut_height: float,
471        view_range: "ViewRange",
472        symbology: "ElementSymbology | None" = None,
473    ) -> list[Union["Line2D", "Arc2D", "Polyline2D", "Hatch2D"]]:
474        """Generate floor plan linework for this wall.
475
476        Creates polylines representing the wall outline, accounting for:
477        - Wall join trim adjustments at corners
478        - Openings from hosted doors and windows
479
480        Args:
481            cut_height: Z coordinate of the section cut
482            view_range: View range parameters
483            symbology: Optional symbology settings (None uses AIA defaults)
484
485        Returns:
486            List of 2D geometry primitives
487        """
488        from bimascode.drawing.hatch_patterns import get_hatch_pattern_for_layer
489        from bimascode.drawing.line_styles import Layer, LineStyle
490        from bimascode.drawing.primitives import Hatch2D, Point2D, Polyline2D
491        from bimascode.drawing.symbology import FillMode, get_default_symbology
492
493        # Use provided symbology or get default
494        if symbology is None:
495            symbology = get_default_symbology("Wall")
496
497        # Check if wall is cut by the section plane
498        bbox = self.get_bounding_box()
499        is_cut = bbox.min_z <= cut_height <= bbox.max_z
500
501        if not is_cut:
502            style = symbology.visible_style or LineStyle.visible()
503        else:
504            style = symbology.cut_style or LineStyle.cut_heavy()
505
506        # Wall geometry
507        start = self.start_point
508        end = self.end_point
509        half_width = self.width / 2.0
510        angle = self.angle
511        wall_length = self.length
512
513        # Direction vectors
514        cos_a = math.cos(angle)
515        sin_a = math.sin(angle)
516
517        # Perpendicular offset vectors (left side is positive)
518        perp_x = -sin_a * half_width
519        perp_y = cos_a * half_width
520
521        # Apply trim adjustments from wall joins
522        # start_offset: negative = extend start backwards, positive = trim start forward
523        # end_offset: positive = extend end forward, negative = trim end back
524        start_offset = 0.0
525        end_offset = 0.0
526        if hasattr(self, "_trim_adjustments") and self._trim_adjustments:
527            start_offset = self._trim_adjustments.get("start_offset", 0.0)
528            end_offset = self._trim_adjustments.get("end_offset", 0.0)
529
530        # Adjusted start and end points along centerline
531        adj_start_x = start[0] + start_offset * cos_a
532        adj_start_y = start[1] + start_offset * sin_a
533        adj_end_x = end[0] + end_offset * cos_a
534        adj_end_y = end[1] + end_offset * sin_a
535
536        # Get miter angles and inside signs for diagonal corner cuts
537        start_miter = self._trim_adjustments.get("start_miter_angle") if self._trim_adjustments else None
538        end_miter = self._trim_adjustments.get("end_miter_angle") if self._trim_adjustments else None
539        start_inside_sign = self._trim_adjustments.get("start_miter_inside_sign", 1) if self._trim_adjustments else 1
540        end_inside_sign = self._trim_adjustments.get("end_miter_inside_sign", 1) if self._trim_adjustments else 1
541
542        # Calculate miter corner offsets for diagonal cuts at L-corners.
543        # The inside corner cuts back, the outside corner extends forward.
544        # This creates a diagonal line on the inside of the L-corner.
545        #
546        # inside_sign indicates which side is inside the L-corner:
547        #   +1 = left side (+perp)
548        #   -1 = right side (-perp)
549
550        # Start miter offsets (positive offset = toward wall end)
551        start_left_offset = 0.0
552        start_right_offset = 0.0
553        if start_miter is not None and abs(start_miter) > 0.01:
554            miter_ext = half_width / math.tan(start_miter)
555            if start_inside_sign > 0:
556                # Inside on left: left cuts back (toward end), right extends back
557                start_left_offset = miter_ext
558                start_right_offset = -miter_ext
559            else:
560                # Inside on right: right cuts back, left extends back
561                start_left_offset = -miter_ext
562                start_right_offset = miter_ext
563
564        # End miter offsets (positive offset = past wall end)
565        end_left_offset = 0.0
566        end_right_offset = 0.0
567        if end_miter is not None and abs(end_miter) > 0.01:
568            miter_ext = half_width / math.tan(end_miter)
569            if end_inside_sign > 0:
570                # Inside on left: left cuts back, right extends forward
571                end_left_offset = -miter_ext
572                end_right_offset = miter_ext
573            else:
574                # Inside on right: right cuts back, left extends forward
575                end_left_offset = miter_ext
576                end_right_offset = -miter_ext
577
578        # Collect openings (doors/windows) that are cut by the section plane
579        openings = []
580        for element in self._hosted_elements:
581            elem_bbox = element.get_bounding_box() if hasattr(element, "get_bounding_box") else None
582            if elem_bbox and elem_bbox.min_z <= cut_height <= elem_bbox.max_z:
583                # Opening is cut - get its position along wall
584                offset = element.offset if hasattr(element, "offset") else 0
585                width = element.width if hasattr(element, "width") else 0
586                openings.append((offset, offset + width))
587
588        # Sort openings by start position
589        openings.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
590
591        result: list[Line2D | Arc2D | Polyline2D | Hatch2D] = []
592
593        # If no openings, draw wall as single polyline
594        if not openings:
595            # Apply miter offsets to corners
596            # Left edge uses +perp, right edge uses -perp
597            corners = [
598                # Start left corner (+perp side)
599                Point2D(
600                    adj_start_x + perp_x + start_left_offset * cos_a,
601                    adj_start_y + perp_y + start_left_offset * sin_a,
602                ),
603                # End left corner (+perp side)
604                Point2D(
605                    adj_end_x + perp_x + end_left_offset * cos_a,
606                    adj_end_y + perp_y + end_left_offset * sin_a,
607                ),
608                # End right corner (-perp side)
609                Point2D(
610                    adj_end_x - perp_x + end_right_offset * cos_a,
611                    adj_end_y - perp_y + end_right_offset * sin_a,
612                ),
613                # Start right corner (-perp side)
614                Point2D(
615                    adj_start_x - perp_x + start_right_offset * cos_a,
616                    adj_start_y - perp_y + start_right_offset * sin_a,
617                ),
618            ]
619            wall_outline = Polyline2D(
620                points=corners,
621                closed=True,
622                style=style,
623                layer=Layer.WALL,
624            )
625            result.append(wall_outline)
626            # Add fill for cut walls based on symbology
627            if is_cut:
628                if symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.SOLID:
629                    # Solid fill hatch using wall outline
630                    solid_hatch = Hatch2D(
631                        boundary=corners,
632                        pattern=None,  # Solid fill
633                        layer=Layer.WALL,
634                        color=symbology.fill_color or (0, 0, 0),
635                    )
636                    result.append(solid_hatch)
637                elif symbology.show_hatching and symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.MATERIAL:
638                    result.extend(
639                        self._generate_layer_hatches(
640                            adj_start_x,
641                            adj_start_y,
642                            adj_end_x,
643                            adj_end_y,
644                            cos_a,
645                            sin_a,
646                            half_width,
647                            get_hatch_pattern_for_layer,
648                        )
649                    )
650        else:
651            # Draw wall segments between openings
652            # Build list of solid segments: (start_offset, end_offset)
653            segments = []
654            current_pos = 0.0
655
656            for open_start, open_end in openings:
657                if open_start > current_pos:
658                    segments.append((current_pos, open_start))
659                current_pos = max(current_pos, open_end)
660
661            # Add final segment after last opening
662            if current_pos < wall_length:
663                segments.append((current_pos, wall_length))
664
665            # Draw each segment as a closed polyline
666            for seg_start, seg_end in segments:
667                # Account for trim adjustments at wall ends
668                actual_start = seg_start
669                actual_end = seg_end
670
671                if seg_start == 0:
672                    actual_start = start_offset  # May be negative (extension)
673                if seg_end == wall_length:
674                    actual_end = wall_length + end_offset
675
676                # Segment corners
677                s_x = start[0] + actual_start * cos_a
678                s_y = start[1] + actual_start * sin_a
679                e_x = start[0] + actual_end * cos_a
680                e_y = start[1] + actual_end * sin_a
681
682                # Apply miter offsets only at actual wall ends (not at openings)
683                seg_start_left = start_left_offset if seg_start == 0 else 0.0
684                seg_start_right = start_right_offset if seg_start == 0 else 0.0
685                seg_end_left = end_left_offset if seg_end == wall_length else 0.0
686                seg_end_right = end_right_offset if seg_end == wall_length else 0.0
687
688                corners = [
689                    Point2D(
690                        s_x + perp_x + seg_start_left * cos_a,
691                        s_y + perp_y + seg_start_left * sin_a,
692                    ),
693                    Point2D(
694                        e_x + perp_x + seg_end_left * cos_a,
695                        e_y + perp_y + seg_end_left * sin_a,
696                    ),
697                    Point2D(
698                        e_x - perp_x + seg_end_right * cos_a,
699                        e_y - perp_y + seg_end_right * sin_a,
700                    ),
701                    Point2D(
702                        s_x - perp_x + seg_start_right * cos_a,
703                        s_y - perp_y + seg_start_right * sin_a,
704                    ),
705                ]
706                seg_outline = Polyline2D(
707                    points=corners,
708                    closed=True,
709                    style=style,
710                    layer=Layer.WALL,
711                )
712                result.append(seg_outline)
713                # Add fill for cut wall segments based on symbology
714                if is_cut:
715                    if symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.SOLID:
716                        # Solid fill hatch using segment outline
717                        solid_hatch = Hatch2D(
718                            boundary=corners,
719                            pattern=None,  # Solid fill
720                            layer=Layer.WALL,
721                            color=symbology.fill_color or (0, 0, 0),
722                        )
723                        result.append(solid_hatch)
724                    elif symbology.show_hatching and symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.MATERIAL:
725                        result.extend(
726                            self._generate_layer_hatches(
727                                s_x,
728                                s_y,
729                                e_x,
730                                e_y,
731                                cos_a,
732                                sin_a,
733                                half_width,
734                                get_hatch_pattern_for_layer,
735                            )
736                        )
737
738        return result

Generate floor plan linework for this wall.

Creates polylines representing the wall outline, accounting for:

  • Wall join trim adjustments at corners
  • Openings from hosted doors and windows

Args: cut_height: Z coordinate of the section cut view_range: View range parameters symbology: Optional symbology settings (None uses AIA defaults)

Returns: List of 2D geometry primitives

class FloorType(bimascode.ElementType): 
 22class FloorType(ElementType):
 23    """
 24    Floor type defining a compound layer stack.
 25
 26    Similar to walls, floors consist of multiple layers stacked
 27    from bottom to top. Total thickness is the sum of all layer thicknesses.
 28    """
 29
 30    def __init__(
 31        self, name: str, layers: list[Layer] | None = None, description: str | None = None
 32    ):
 33        """
 34        Create a floor type.
 35
 36        Args:
 37            name: Name for this floor type
 38            layers: List of layers from bottom to top
 39            description: Optional description
 40        """
 41        super().__init__(name)
 42        self.layers = layers or []
 43        self.description = description
 44
 45        # Store total thickness as a parameter
 46        self._update_thickness()
 47
 48    def add_layer(
 49        self,
 50        material: Material,
 51        thickness: Length | float,
 52        function: LayerFunction = LayerFunction.OTHER,
 53        structural: bool = False,
 54        position: int | None = None,
 55    ) -> Layer:
 56        """
 57        Add a material layer to the floor assembly.
 58
 59        Args:
 60            material: Material for this layer
 61            thickness: Layer thickness
 62            function: Functional role of this layer
 63            structural: Whether layer is load-bearing
 64            position: Position to insert layer (0=bottom, None=top)
 65
 66        Returns:
 67            The created Layer object
 68        """
 69        layer = Layer(material, thickness, function, structural)
 70
 71        if position is None:
 72            self.layers.append(layer)
 73        else:
 74            self.layers.insert(position, layer)
 75
 76        self._update_thickness()
 77        return layer
 78
 79    def remove_layer(self, index: int) -> None:
 80        """
 81        Remove a layer by index.
 82
 83        Args:
 84            index: Layer index (0 = bottom)
 85        """
 86        if 0 <= index < len(self.layers):
 87            self.layers.pop(index)
 88            self._update_thickness()
 89
 90    def get_layer(self, index: int) -> Layer:
 91        """
 92        Get a layer by index.
 93
 94        Args:
 95            index: Layer index (0 = bottom)
 96
 97        Returns:
 98            Layer at the specified index
 99        """
100        return self.layers[index]
101
102    @property
103    def layer_count(self) -> int:
104        """Get the number of layers."""
105        return len(self.layers)
106
107    @property
108    def total_thickness(self) -> Length:
109        """Get the total floor thickness (sum of all layers)."""
110        return Length(sum(layer.thickness_mm for layer in self.layers), "mm")
111
112    @property
113    def total_thickness_mm(self) -> float:
114        """Get total floor thickness in millimeters."""
115        return self.total_thickness.mm
116
117    def _update_thickness(self) -> None:
118        """Update the thickness parameter based on layer thicknesses."""
119        self.set_parameter("thickness", self.total_thickness_mm)
120
121    def get_structural_layers(self) -> list[Layer]:
122        """Get all structural layers."""
123        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.structural]
124
125    def get_layers_by_function(self, function: LayerFunction) -> list[Layer]:
126        """
127        Get all layers with a specific function.
128
129        Args:
130            function: Layer function to filter by
131
132        Returns:
133            List of layers with the specified function
134        """
135        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.function == function]
136
137    def create_geometry(self, instance: "Floor") -> Compound:
138        """
139        Create 3D geometry for a floor instance.
140
141        The floor is created as a compound of extruded polygons,
142        one for each layer. Layers are stacked from bottom to top.
143
144        Args:
145            instance: Floor instance to create geometry for
146
147        Returns:
148            build123d Compound representing the floor
149        """
150
151        # Get floor parameters
152        boundary = instance.get_parameter("boundary")
153
154        if boundary is None:
155            raise ValueError("Floor must have a boundary polygon")
156
157        # Create layer geometries
158        layer_solids = []
159        current_z = 0.0
160
161        for layer in self.layers:
162            # Extrude the boundary polygon for this layer
163            # For now, simple extrusion (slope support can be added later)
164            poly = Polygon(*boundary)
165            layer_solid = extrude(poly, amount=layer.thickness_mm)
166
167            # Position the layer vertically
168            layer_solid = layer_solid.locate(Location((0, 0, current_z)))
169            layer_solids.append(layer_solid)
170
171            current_z += layer.thickness_mm
172
173        # Create floor compound
174        floor_compound = Compound(children=layer_solids)
175
176        # Apply boolean subtraction for openings
177        if hasattr(instance, "openings") and instance.openings:
178            for opening in instance.openings:
179                try:
180                    opening_geom = opening.get_opening_geometry()
181                    floor_compound = floor_compound - opening_geom
182                except Exception:
183                    # Boolean operation failed - skip this opening
184                    pass
185
186        return floor_compound
187
188    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file):
189        """
190        Export floor type to IFC as IfcMaterialLayerSet.
191
192        Args:
193            ifc_file: IFC file object
194
195        Returns:
196            IfcMaterialLayerSet
197        """
198        # Create IFC material layers
199        ifc_layers = []
200        for i, layer in enumerate(self.layers):
201            # Create IFC material for layer
202            ifc_material = layer.material.to_ifc(ifc_file)
203
204            # Create material layer
205            ifc_layer = ifc_file.create_entity(
206                "IfcMaterialLayer",
207                Material=ifc_material,
208                LayerThickness=layer.thickness_mm,
209                IsVentilated=False,
210                Name=layer.description,
211                Category=layer.function.value,
212                Priority=i + 1,
213            )
214            ifc_layers.append(ifc_layer)
215
216        # Create material layer set
217        ifc_layer_set = ifc_file.create_entity(
218            "IfcMaterialLayerSet",
219            MaterialLayers=ifc_layers,
220            LayerSetName=self.name,
221            Description=self.description,
222        )
223
224        return ifc_layer_set
225
226    def __repr__(self) -> str:
227        return f"FloorType(name='{self.name}', layers={self.layer_count}, thickness={self.total_thickness_mm:.1f}mm)"

Floor type defining a compound layer stack.

Similar to walls, floors consist of multiple layers stacked from bottom to top. Total thickness is the sum of all layer thicknesses.

FloorType( name: str, layers: list[Layer] | None = None, description: str | None = None) 
30    def __init__(
31        self, name: str, layers: list[Layer] | None = None, description: str | None = None
32    ):
33        """
34        Create a floor type.
35
36        Args:
37            name: Name for this floor type
38            layers: List of layers from bottom to top
39            description: Optional description
40        """
41        super().__init__(name)
42        self.layers = layers or []
43        self.description = description
44
45        # Store total thickness as a parameter
46        self._update_thickness()

Create a floor type.

Args: name: Name for this floor type layers: List of layers from bottom to top description: Optional description

layers
description
def add_layer( self, material: bimascode.utils.materials.Material, thickness: Length | float, function: LayerFunction = <LayerFunction.OTHER: 'Other'>, structural: bool = False, position: int | None = None) -> Layer: 
48    def add_layer(
49        self,
50        material: Material,
51        thickness: Length | float,
52        function: LayerFunction = LayerFunction.OTHER,
53        structural: bool = False,
54        position: int | None = None,
55    ) -> Layer:
56        """
57        Add a material layer to the floor assembly.
58
59        Args:
60            material: Material for this layer
61            thickness: Layer thickness
62            function: Functional role of this layer
63            structural: Whether layer is load-bearing
64            position: Position to insert layer (0=bottom, None=top)
65
66        Returns:
67            The created Layer object
68        """
69        layer = Layer(material, thickness, function, structural)
70
71        if position is None:
72            self.layers.append(layer)
73        else:
74            self.layers.insert(position, layer)
75
76        self._update_thickness()
77        return layer

Add a material layer to the floor assembly.

Args: material: Material for this layer thickness: Layer thickness function: Functional role of this layer structural: Whether layer is load-bearing position: Position to insert layer (0=bottom, None=top)

Returns: The created Layer object

def remove_layer(self, index: int) -> None: 
79    def remove_layer(self, index: int) -> None:
80        """
81        Remove a layer by index.
82
83        Args:
84            index: Layer index (0 = bottom)
85        """
86        if 0 <= index < len(self.layers):
87            self.layers.pop(index)
88            self._update_thickness()

def get_layer(self, index: int) -> Layer: 
 90    def get_layer(self, index: int) -> Layer:
 91        """
 92        Get a layer by index.
 93
 94        Args:
 95            index: Layer index (0 = bottom)
 96
 97        Returns:
 98            Layer at the specified index
 99        """
100        return self.layers[index]

102    @property
103    def layer_count(self) -> int:
104        """Get the number of layers."""
105        return len(self.layers)

Get the number of layers.

107    @property
