112    @property

114        """Get total floor thickness in millimeters."""
115        return self.total_thickness.mm

Get total floor thickness in millimeters.

def get_structural_layers(self) -> list[Layer]: 
121    def get_structural_layers(self) -> list[Layer]:
122        """Get all structural layers."""
123        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.structural]

Get all structural layers.

def get_layers_by_function( self, function: LayerFunction) -> list[Layer]: 
125    def get_layers_by_function(self, function: LayerFunction) -> list[Layer]:
126        """
127        Get all layers with a specific function.
128
129        Args:
130            function: Layer function to filter by
131
132        Returns:
133            List of layers with the specified function
134        """
135        return [layer for layer in self.layers if layer.function == function]

Get all layers with a specific function.

Args: function: Layer function to filter by

Returns: List of layers with the specified function

def create_geometry( self, instance: Floor) -> build123d.topology.composite.Compound: 
137    def create_geometry(self, instance: "Floor") -> Compound:
138        """
139        Create 3D geometry for a floor instance.
140
141        The floor is created as a compound of extruded polygons,
142        one for each layer. Layers are stacked from bottom to top.
143
144        Args:
145            instance: Floor instance to create geometry for
146
147        Returns:
148            build123d Compound representing the floor
149        """
150
151        # Get floor parameters
152        boundary = instance.get_parameter("boundary")
153
154        if boundary is None:
155            raise ValueError("Floor must have a boundary polygon")
156
157        # Create layer geometries
158        layer_solids = []
159        current_z = 0.0
160
161        for layer in self.layers:
162            # Extrude the boundary polygon for this layer
163            # For now, simple extrusion (slope support can be added later)
164            poly = Polygon(*boundary)
165            layer_solid = extrude(poly, amount=layer.thickness_mm)
166
167            # Position the layer vertically
168            layer_solid = layer_solid.locate(Location((0, 0, current_z)))
169            layer_solids.append(layer_solid)
170
171            current_z += layer.thickness_mm
172
173        # Create floor compound
174        floor_compound = Compound(children=layer_solids)
175
176        # Apply boolean subtraction for openings
177        if hasattr(instance, "openings") and instance.openings:
178            for opening in instance.openings:
179                try:
180                    opening_geom = opening.get_opening_geometry()
181                    floor_compound = floor_compound - opening_geom
182                except Exception:
183                    # Boolean operation failed - skip this opening
184                    pass
185
186        return floor_compound

Create 3D geometry for a floor instance.

The floor is created as a compound of extruded polygons, one for each layer. Layers are stacked from bottom to top.

Args: instance: Floor instance to create geometry for

Returns: build123d Compound representing the floor

def to_ifc(self, ifc_file): 
188    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file):
189        """
190        Export floor type to IFC as IfcMaterialLayerSet.
191
192        Args:
193            ifc_file: IFC file object
194
195        Returns:
196            IfcMaterialLayerSet
197        """
198        # Create IFC material layers
199        ifc_layers = []
200        for i, layer in enumerate(self.layers):
201            # Create IFC material for layer
202            ifc_material = layer.material.to_ifc(ifc_file)
203
204            # Create material layer
205            ifc_layer = ifc_file.create_entity(
206                "IfcMaterialLayer",
207                Material=ifc_material,
208                LayerThickness=layer.thickness_mm,
209                IsVentilated=False,
210                Name=layer.description,
211                Category=layer.function.value,
212                Priority=i + 1,
213            )
214            ifc_layers.append(ifc_layer)
215
216        # Create material layer set
217        ifc_layer_set = ifc_file.create_entity(
218            "IfcMaterialLayerSet",
219            MaterialLayers=ifc_layers,
220            LayerSetName=self.name,
221            Description=self.description,
222        )
223
224        return ifc_layer_set

Export floor type to IFC as IfcMaterialLayerSet.

Args: ifc_file: IFC file object

Returns: IfcMaterialLayerSet

class Floor(bimascode.ElementInstance, bimascode.core.world_geometry.FreestandingElementMixin): 
 26class Floor(ElementInstance, FreestandingElementMixin):
 27    """
 28    A floor/slab element.
 29
 30    Floors are horizontal elements defined by a boundary polygon.
 31    They belong to a level and extend upward by their thickness.
 32    """
 33
 34    def __init__(
 35        self,
 36        floor_type: "FloorType",
 37        boundary: list[tuple[float, float]],
 38        level: Level,
 39        slope: float = 0.0,
 40        structural: bool = False,
 41        name: str | None = None,
 42    ):
 43        """
 44        Create a floor.
 45
 46        Args:
 47            floor_type: FloorType defining the floor assembly
 48            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates defining floor boundary
 49            level: Level this floor sits on
 50            slope: Floor slope in degrees (for drainage, ramps, etc.)
 51            structural: If True, floor is a structural slab (base slab)
 52            name: Optional name for this floor
 53        """
 54        super().__init__(floor_type, name)
 55
 56        self.level = level
 57
 58        # Openings in this floor
 59        self._openings: list[Opening] = []
 60
 61        # Structural flag
 62        self._structural = structural
 63
 64        # Store geometric parameters
 65        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
 66        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
 67
 68        # Register with level
 69        if hasattr(level, "add_element"):
 70            level.add_element(self)
 71
 72    @property
 73    def boundary(self) -> list[tuple[float, float]]:
 74        """Get floor boundary polygon."""
 75        return self.get_parameter("boundary")
 76
 77    @property
 78    def slope(self) -> float:
 79        """Get floor slope in degrees."""
 80        return self.get_parameter("slope")
 81
 82    @property
 83    def structural(self) -> bool:
 84        """Check if floor is marked as structural (base slab)."""
 85        return self._structural
 86
 87    @structural.setter
 88    def structural(self, value: bool) -> None:
 89        """Set the structural flag."""
 90        self._structural = value
 91
 92    @property
 93    def thickness(self) -> float:
 94        """Get floor thickness in millimeters."""
 95        return self.get_parameter("thickness")
 96
 97    @property
 98    def thickness_length(self) -> Length:
 99        """Get floor thickness as Length object."""
100        return Length(self.thickness, "mm")
101
102    @property
103    def area(self) -> float:
104        """
105        Calculate floor area in square millimeters.
106
107        Uses the Shoelace formula for polygon area.
108        """
109        boundary = self.boundary
110        if len(boundary) < 3:
111            return 0.0
112
113        # Shoelace formula
114        area = 0.0
115        n = len(boundary)
116        for i in range(n):
117            j = (i + 1) % n
118            area += boundary[i][0] * boundary[j][1]
119            area -= boundary[j][0] * boundary[i][1]
120
121        return abs(area) / 2.0
122
123    @property
124    def area_m2(self) -> float:
125        """Get floor area in square meters."""
126        return self.area / 1_000_000.0
127
128    def get_centroid(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
129        """
130        Calculate the centroid of the floor boundary.
131
132        Returns:
133            (x, y) coordinates of centroid
134        """
135        boundary = self.boundary
136        if len(boundary) == 0:
137            return (0.0, 0.0)
138
139        x_sum = sum(p[0] for p in boundary)
140        y_sum = sum(p[1] for p in boundary)
141        n = len(boundary)
142
143        return (x_sum / n, y_sum / n)
144
145    def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
146        """
147        Get the 3D center point of the floor.
148
149        Returns:
150            (x, y, z) coordinates of floor center
151        """
152        cx, cy = self.get_centroid()
153        z = self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness / 2
154        return (cx, cy, z)
155
156    def _get_world_position(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
157        """Get world position for floor geometry.
158
159        build123d's Polygon centers vertices at the centroid, so we translate
160        by the centroid to restore correct world positioning.
161
162        Returns:
163            (x, y, z) at floor centroid, with Z at level elevation
164        """
165        cx, cy = self.get_centroid()
166        return (cx, cy, self.level.elevation_mm)
167
168    def _get_world_rotation(self) -> float:
169        """Get world rotation for floor geometry.
170
171        Floors have no rotation (horizontal elements).
172
173        Returns:
174            0.0 (no rotation)
175        """
176        return 0.0
177
178    def set_boundary(self, boundary: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> None:
179        """
180        Set the floor boundary.
181
182        Args:
183            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates
184        """
185        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
186        self.invalidate_geometry()
187
188    def set_slope(self, slope: float) -> None:
189        """
190        Set the floor slope.
191
192        Args:
193            slope: Slope in degrees
194        """
195        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
196        self.invalidate_geometry()
197
198    @property
199    def openings(self) -> list["Opening"]:
200        """Get all openings in this floor."""
201        return self._openings.copy()
202
203    def add_opening(
204        self, opening_boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], name: str | None = None
205    ) -> "Opening":
206        """
207        Add an opening (void) in the floor.
208
209        Args:
210            opening_boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates for opening
211            name: Optional name for the opening
212
213        Returns:
214            The created Opening object
215        """
216        from bimascode.architecture.opening import Opening
217
218        opening = Opening(
219            host_element=self,
220            boundary=opening_boundary,
221            depth=self.thickness + 2,  # +2mm for clean cut
222            name=name or f"Opening_{len(self._openings) + 1}",
223        )
224        self._openings.append(opening)
225        self.invalidate_geometry()
226        return opening
227
228    def remove_opening(self, opening: "Opening") -> None:
229        """
230        Remove an opening from this floor.
231
232        Args:
233            opening: Opening to remove
234        """
235        if opening in self._openings:
236            self._openings.remove(opening)
237            self.invalidate_geometry()
238
239    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey):
240        """
241        Export floor to IFC.
242
243        Args:
244            ifc_file: IFC file object
245            ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place floor in
246
247        Returns:
248            IfcSlab entity
249        """
250        # Determine predefined type based on structural flag
251        predefined_type = "BASESLAB" if self._structural else "FLOOR"
252
253        # Create slab
254        ifc_slab = ifc_file.create_entity(
255            "IfcSlab",
256            GlobalId=self.guid,
257            Name=self.name,
258            Description=f"{self.type.name} floor",
259            PredefinedType=predefined_type,
260        )
261
262        # Set placement (at level elevation)
263        centroid = self.get_centroid()
264        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((float(centroid[0]), float(centroid[1]), 0.0))
265
266        axis_placement = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(
267            location,
268            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0)),
269            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((1.0, 0.0, 0.0)),
270        )
271
272        local_placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
273            ifc_building_storey.ObjectPlacement, axis_placement
274        )
275
276        ifc_slab.ObjectPlacement = local_placement
277
278        # Create geometry representation using BREP
279        geom = self.get_geometry()
280
281        if geom:
282            from bimascode.export.ifc_geometry import build123d_to_ifc_brep
283
284            # Convert build123d geometry to IFC BREP
285            ifc_brep = build123d_to_ifc_brep(geom, ifc_file)
286
287            if ifc_brep:
288                # Create shape representation with BREP
289                shape_representation = ifc_file.createIfcShapeRepresentation(
290                    ifc_file.by_type("IfcGeometricRepresentationContext")[0],
291                    "Body",
292                    "Brep",
293                    [ifc_brep],
294                )
295
296                # Create product definition shape
297                product_shape = ifc_file.createIfcProductDefinitionShape(
298                    None, None, [shape_representation]
299                )
300
301                ifc_slab.Representation = product_shape
302
303        # Associate with building storey
304        ifc_file.createIfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure(
305            self._generate_guid(),
306            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
307            f"Floor{self.name}Container",
308            None,
309            [ifc_slab],
310            ifc_building_storey,
311        )
312
313        # Associate with material layer set
314        material_layer_set = self.type.to_ifc(ifc_file)
315
316        ifc_file.createIfcRelAssociatesMaterial(
317            self._generate_guid(),
318            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
319            None,
320            None,
321            [ifc_slab],
322            material_layer_set,
323        )
324
325        return ifc_slab
326
327    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox:
328        """Get axis-aligned bounding box for this floor.
329
330        Returns:
331            BoundingBox encompassing the floor geometry
332        """
333        return BoundingBox.from_polygon_2d(
334            self.boundary, self.level.elevation_mm, self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness
335        )
336
337    def get_plan_representation(
338        self,
339        cut_height: float,
340        view_range: "ViewRange",
341        symbology: "ElementSymbology | None" = None,
342    ) -> list[Union["Line2D", "Arc2D", "Polyline2D", "Hatch2D"]]:
343        """Generate floor plan linework for this floor.
344
345        Floors are typically shown with their boundary outline.
346
347        Args:
348            cut_height: Z coordinate of the section cut
349            view_range: View range parameters
350            symbology: Optional symbology settings (None uses AIA defaults)
351
352        Returns:
353            List of 2D geometry primitives
354        """
355        from bimascode.drawing.hatch_patterns import get_hatch_pattern_for_layer
356        from bimascode.drawing.line_styles import Layer, LineStyle
357        from bimascode.drawing.primitives import Hatch2D, Point2D, Polyline2D
358        from bimascode.drawing.symbology import FillMode, get_default_symbology
359
360        # Use provided symbology or get default
361        if symbology is None:
362            symbology = get_default_symbology("Floor")
363
364        result: list[Line2D | Arc2D | Polyline2D | Hatch2D] = []
365
366        # Floors are below the cut plane - show with visible (not cut) style
367        bbox = self.get_bounding_box()
368        is_cut = bbox.min_z <= cut_height <= bbox.max_z
369
370        if is_cut:
371            style = symbology.cut_style or LineStyle.cut_medium()
372        else:
373            style = symbology.visible_style or LineStyle.visible()
374
375        # Create polyline from boundary
376        boundary = self.boundary
377        if len(boundary) >= 3:
378            points = [Point2D(p[0], p[1]) for p in boundary]
379            floor_outline = Polyline2D(
380                points=points,
381                closed=True,
382                style=style,
383                layer=Layer.FLOOR,
384            )
385            result.append(floor_outline)
386
387            # Add per-layer hatches when floor is cut if symbology allows
388            if is_cut and symbology.show_hatching and symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.MATERIAL:
389                for layer in self.type.layers:
390                    pattern = get_hatch_pattern_for_layer(layer)
391                    hatch = Hatch2D(
392                        boundary=points,
393                        pattern=pattern.name,
394                        scale=pattern.scale,
395                        rotation=pattern.rotation,
396                        color=pattern.color,
397                        layer=Layer.FLOOR,
398                    )
399                    result.append(hatch)
400
401        return result
402
403    def __repr__(self) -> str:
404        return (
405            f"Floor(name='{self.name}', type='{self.type.name}', "
406            f"area={self.area_m2:.2f}m², thickness={self.thickness:.1f}mm)"
407        )

A floor/slab element.

Floors are horizontal elements defined by a boundary polygon. They belong to a level and extend upward by their thickness.

Floor( floor_type: FloorType, boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], level: Level, slope: float = 0.0, structural: bool = False, name: str | None = None) 
34    def __init__(
35        self,
36        floor_type: "FloorType",
37        boundary: list[tuple[float, float]],
38        level: Level,
39        slope: float = 0.0,
40        structural: bool = False,
41        name: str | None = None,
42    ):
43        """
44        Create a floor.
45
46        Args:
47            floor_type: FloorType defining the floor assembly
48            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates defining floor boundary
49            level: Level this floor sits on
50            slope: Floor slope in degrees (for drainage, ramps, etc.)
51            structural: If True, floor is a structural slab (base slab)
52            name: Optional name for this floor
53        """
54        super().__init__(floor_type, name)
55
56        self.level = level
57
58        # Openings in this floor
59        self._openings: list[Opening] = []
60
61        # Structural flag
62        self._structural = structural
63
64        # Store geometric parameters
65        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
66        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
67
68        # Register with level
69        if hasattr(level, "add_element"):
70            level.add_element(self)

Create a floor.

Args: floor_type: FloorType defining the floor assembly boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates defining floor boundary level: Level this floor sits on slope: Floor slope in degrees (for drainage, ramps, etc.) structural: If True, floor is a structural slab (base slab) name: Optional name for this floor

level
boundary: list[tuple[float, float]] 
72    @property
73    def boundary(self) -> list[tuple[float, float]]:
74        """Get floor boundary polygon."""
75        return self.get_parameter("boundary")

Get floor boundary polygon.

slope: float 
77    @property
78    def slope(self) -> float:
79        """Get floor slope in degrees."""
80        return self.get_parameter("slope")

Get floor slope in degrees.

structural: bool 
82    @property
83    def structural(self) -> bool:
84        """Check if floor is marked as structural (base slab)."""
85        return self._structural

Check if floor is marked as structural (base slab).

thickness: float 
92    @property
93    def thickness(self) -> float:
94        """Get floor thickness in millimeters."""
95        return self.get_parameter("thickness")

Get floor thickness in millimeters.

thickness_length: Length 
 97    @property
 98    def thickness_length(self) -> Length:
 99        """Get floor thickness as Length object."""
100        return Length(self.thickness, "mm")

Get floor thickness as Length object.

area: float 
102    @property
103    def area(self) -> float:
104        """
105        Calculate floor area in square millimeters.
106
107        Uses the Shoelace formula for polygon area.
108        """
109        boundary = self.boundary
110        if len(boundary) < 3:
111            return 0.0
112
113        # Shoelace formula
114        area = 0.0
115        n = len(boundary)
116        for i in range(n):
117            j = (i + 1) % n
118            area += boundary[i][0] * boundary[j][1]
119            area -= boundary[j][0] * boundary[i][1]
120
121        return abs(area) / 2.0

Calculate floor area in square millimeters.

Uses the Shoelace formula for polygon area.

area_m2: float 
123    @property
124    def area_m2(self) -> float:
125        """Get floor area in square meters."""
126        return self.area / 1_000_000.0

Get floor area in square meters.

def get_centroid(self) -> tuple[float, float]: 
128    def get_centroid(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
129        """
130        Calculate the centroid of the floor boundary.
131
132        Returns:
133            (x, y) coordinates of centroid
134        """
135        boundary = self.boundary
136        if len(boundary) == 0:
137            return (0.0, 0.0)
138
139        x_sum = sum(p[0] for p in boundary)
140        y_sum = sum(p[1] for p in boundary)
141        n = len(boundary)
142
143        return (x_sum / n, y_sum / n)

Calculate the centroid of the floor boundary.

Returns: (x, y) coordinates of centroid

def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]: 
145    def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
146        """
147        Get the 3D center point of the floor.
148
149        Returns:
150            (x, y, z) coordinates of floor center
151        """
152        cx, cy = self.get_centroid()
153        z = self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness / 2
154        return (cx, cy, z)

Get the 3D center point of the floor.

Returns: (x, y, z) coordinates of floor center

def set_boundary(self, boundary: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> None: 
178    def set_boundary(self, boundary: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> None:
179        """
180        Set the floor boundary.
181
182        Args:
183            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates
184        """
185        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
186        self.invalidate_geometry()

Set the floor boundary.

Args: boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates

def set_slope(self, slope: float) -> None: 
188    def set_slope(self, slope: float) -> None:
189        """
190        Set the floor slope.
191
192        Args:
193            slope: Slope in degrees
194        """
195        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
196        self.invalidate_geometry()

Set the floor slope.

Args: slope: Slope in degrees

openings: list[bimascode.architecture.opening.Opening] 
198    @property
199    def openings(self) -> list["Opening"]:
200        """Get all openings in this floor."""
201        return self._openings.copy()

Get all openings in this floor.

def add_opening( self, opening_boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], name: str | None = None) -> bimascode.architecture.opening.Opening: 
203    def add_opening(
204        self, opening_boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], name: str | None = None
205    ) -> "Opening":
206        """
207        Add an opening (void) in the floor.
208
209        Args:
210            opening_boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates for opening
211            name: Optional name for the opening
212
213        Returns:
214            The created Opening object
215        """
216        from bimascode.architecture.opening import Opening
217
218        opening = Opening(
219            host_element=self,
220            boundary=opening_boundary,
221            depth=self.thickness + 2,  # +2mm for clean cut
222            name=name or f"Opening_{len(self._openings) + 1}",
223        )
224        self._openings.append(opening)
225        self.invalidate_geometry()
226        return opening

Add an opening (void) in the floor.

Args: opening_boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates for opening name: Optional name for the opening

Returns: The created Opening object

def remove_opening(self, opening: bimascode.architecture.opening.Opening) -> None: 
228    def remove_opening(self, opening: "Opening") -> None:
229        """
230        Remove an opening from this floor.
231
232        Args:
233            opening: Opening to remove
234        """
235        if opening in self._openings:
236            self._openings.remove(opening)
237            self.invalidate_geometry()

Remove an opening from this floor.

Args: opening: Opening to remove

def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey): 
239    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey):
240        """
241        Export floor to IFC.
242
243        Args:
244            ifc_file: IFC file object
245            ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place floor in
246
247        Returns:
248            IfcSlab entity
249        """
250        # Determine predefined type based on structural flag
251        predefined_type = "BASESLAB" if self._structural else "FLOOR"
252
253        # Create slab
254        ifc_slab = ifc_file.create_entity(
255            "IfcSlab",
256            GlobalId=self.guid,
257            Name=self.name,
258            Description=f"{self.type.name} floor",
259            PredefinedType=predefined_type,
260        )
261
262        # Set placement (at level elevation)
263        centroid = self.get_centroid()
264        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((float(centroid[0]), float(centroid[1]), 0.0))
265
266        axis_placement = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(
267            location,
268            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0)),
269            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((1.0, 0.0, 0.0)),
270        )
271
272        local_placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
273            ifc_building_storey.ObjectPlacement, axis_placement
274        )
275
276        ifc_slab.ObjectPlacement = local_placement
277
278        # Create geometry representation using BREP
279        geom = self.get_geometry()
280
281        if geom:
282            from bimascode.export.ifc_geometry import build123d_to_ifc_brep
283
284            # Convert build123d geometry to IFC BREP
285            ifc_brep = build123d_to_ifc_brep(geom, ifc_file)
286
287            if ifc_brep:
288                # Create shape representation with BREP
289                shape_representation = ifc_file.createIfcShapeRepresentation(
290                    ifc_file.by_type("IfcGeometricRepresentationContext")[0],
291                    "Body",
292                    "Brep",
293                    [ifc_brep],
294                )
295
296                # Create product definition shape
297                product_shape = ifc_file.createIfcProductDefinitionShape(
298                    None, None, [shape_representation]
299                )
300
301                ifc_slab.Representation = product_shape
302
303        # Associate with building storey
304        ifc_file.createIfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure(
305            self._generate_guid(),
306            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
307            f"Floor{self.name}Container",
308            None,
309            [ifc_slab],
310            ifc_building_storey,
311        )
312
313        # Associate with material layer set
314        material_layer_set = self.type.to_ifc(ifc_file)
315
316        ifc_file.createIfcRelAssociatesMaterial(
317            self._generate_guid(),
318            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
319            None,
320            None,
321            [ifc_slab],
322            material_layer_set,
323        )
324
325        return ifc_slab

Export floor to IFC.

Args: ifc_file: IFC file object ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place floor in

Returns: IfcSlab entity

def get_bounding_box(self) -> bimascode.performance.bounding_box.BoundingBox: 
327    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox:
328        """Get axis-aligned bounding box for this floor.
329
330        Returns:
331            BoundingBox encompassing the floor geometry
332        """
333        return BoundingBox.from_polygon_2d(
334            self.boundary, self.level.elevation_mm, self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness
335        )

Get axis-aligned bounding box for this floor.

Returns: BoundingBox encompassing the floor geometry

def get_plan_representation( self, cut_height: float, view_range: bimascode.drawing.view_base.ViewRange, symbology: bimascode.drawing.symbology.ElementSymbology | None = None) -> list[typing.Union[bimascode.drawing.primitives.Line2D, bimascode.drawing.primitives.Arc2D, bimascode.drawing.primitives.Polyline2D, bimascode.drawing.primitives.Hatch2D]]: 
337    def get_plan_representation(
338        self,
339        cut_height: float,
340        view_range: "ViewRange",
341        symbology: "ElementSymbology | None" = None,
342    ) -> list[Union["Line2D", "Arc2D", "Polyline2D", "Hatch2D"]]:
343        """Generate floor plan linework for this floor.
344
345        Floors are typically shown with their boundary outline.
346
347        Args:
348            cut_height: Z coordinate of the section cut
349            view_range: View range parameters
350            symbology: Optional symbology settings (None uses AIA defaults)
351
352        Returns:
353            List of 2D geometry primitives
354        """
355        from bimascode.drawing.hatch_patterns import get_hatch_pattern_for_layer
356        from bimascode.drawing.line_styles import Layer, LineStyle
357        from bimascode.drawing.primitives import Hatch2D, Point2D, Polyline2D
358        from bimascode.drawing.symbology import FillMode, get_default_symbology
359
360        # Use provided symbology or get default
361        if symbology is None:
362            symbology = get_default_symbology("Floor")
363
364        result: list[Line2D | Arc2D | Polyline2D | Hatch2D] = []
365
366        # Floors are below the cut plane - show with visible (not cut) style
367        bbox = self.get_bounding_box()
368        is_cut = bbox.min_z <= cut_height <= bbox.max_z
369
370        if is_cut:
371            style = symbology.cut_style or LineStyle.cut_medium()
372        else:
373            style = symbology.visible_style or LineStyle.visible()
374
375        # Create polyline from boundary
376        boundary = self.boundary
377        if len(boundary) >= 3:
378            points = [Point2D(p[0], p[1]) for p in boundary]
379            floor_outline = Polyline2D(
380                points=points,
381                closed=True,
382                style=style,
383                layer=Layer.FLOOR,
384            )
385            result.append(floor_outline)
386
387            # Add per-layer hatches when floor is cut if symbology allows
388            if is_cut and symbology.show_hatching and symbology.fill_mode == FillMode.MATERIAL:
389                for layer in self.type.layers:
390                    pattern = get_hatch_pattern_for_layer(layer)
391                    hatch = Hatch2D(
392                        boundary=points,
393                        pattern=pattern.name,
394                        scale=pattern.scale,
395                        rotation=pattern.rotation,
396                        color=pattern.color,
397                        layer=Layer.FLOOR,
398                    )
399                    result.append(hatch)
400
401        return result

Generate floor plan linework for this floor.

Floors are typically shown with their boundary outline.

Args: cut_height: Z coordinate of the section cut view_range: View range parameters symbology: Optional symbology settings (None uses AIA defaults)

Returns: List of 2D geometry primitives

class Roof(bimascode.ElementInstance): 
 22class Roof(ElementInstance):
 23    """
 24    A flat roof element.
 25
 26    Roofs are similar to floors but located at the top of a building.
 27    They support slope for drainage (typically 1-2%).
 28
 29    Note: Sprint 2 supports flat roofs only. Pitched roofs are P1.
 30    """
 31
 32    def __init__(
 33        self,
 34        roof_type: FloorType,  # Reuse FloorType for roof assemblies
 35        boundary: list[tuple[float, float]],
 36        level: Level,
 37        slope: float = 0.0,
 38        name: str | None = None,
 39    ):
 40        """
 41        Create a flat roof.
 42
 43        Args:
 44            roof_type: FloorType defining the roof assembly
 45            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates defining roof boundary
 46            level: Level this roof sits on
 47            slope: Roof slope in degrees (for drainage, typically 1-2%)
 48            name: Optional name for this roof
 49        """
 50        super().__init__(roof_type, name)
 51
 52        self.level = level
 53
 54        # Openings in this roof
 55        self._openings: list[Opening] = []
 56
 57        # Store geometric parameters
 58        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
 59        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
 60
 61        # Register with level
 62        if hasattr(level, "add_element"):
 63            level.add_element(self)
 64
 65    @property
 66    def boundary(self) -> list[tuple[float, float]]:
 67        """Get roof boundary polygon."""
 68        return self.get_parameter("boundary")
 69
 70    @property
 71    def slope(self) -> float:
 72        """Get roof slope in degrees."""
 73        return self.get_parameter("slope")
 74
 75    @property
 76    def thickness(self) -> float:
 77        """Get roof thickness in millimeters."""
 78        return self.get_parameter("thickness")
 79
 80    @property
 81    def thickness_length(self) -> Length:
 82        """Get roof thickness as Length object."""
 83        return Length(self.thickness, "mm")
 84
 85    @property
 86    def area(self) -> float:
 87        """
 88        Calculate roof area in square millimeters.
 89
 90        Uses the Shoelace formula for polygon area.
 91        """
 92        boundary = self.boundary
 93        if len(boundary) < 3:
 94            return 0.0
 95
 96        # Shoelace formula
 97        area = 0.0
 98        n = len(boundary)
 99        for i in range(n):
100            j = (i + 1) % n
101            area += boundary[i][0] * boundary[j][1]
102            area -= boundary[j][0] * boundary[i][1]
103
104        return abs(area) / 2.0
105
106    @property
107    def area_m2(self) -> float:
108        """Get roof area in square meters."""
109        return self.area / 1_000_000.0
110
111    def get_centroid(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
112        """
113        Calculate the centroid of the roof boundary.
114
115        Returns:
116            (x, y) coordinates of centroid
117        """
118        boundary = self.boundary
119        if len(boundary) == 0:
120            return (0.0, 0.0)
121
122        x_sum = sum(p[0] for p in boundary)
123        y_sum = sum(p[1] for p in boundary)
124        n = len(boundary)
125
126        return (x_sum / n, y_sum / n)
127
128    def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
129        """
130        Get the 3D center point of the roof.
131
132        Returns:
133            (x, y, z) coordinates of roof center
134        """
135        cx, cy = self.get_centroid()
136        z = self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness / 2
137        return (cx, cy, z)
138
139    def set_boundary(self, boundary: list[tuple[float, float]]) -> None:
140        """
141        Set the roof boundary.
142
143        Args:
144            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates
145        """
146        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
147        self.invalidate_geometry()
148
149    def set_slope(self, slope: float) -> None:
150        """
151        Set the roof slope.
152
153        Args:
154            slope: Slope in degrees (typical drainage slope is 1-2 degrees)
155        """
156        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
157        self.invalidate_geometry()
158
159    @property
160    def openings(self) -> list["Opening"]:
161        """Get all openings in this roof."""
162        return self._openings.copy()
163
164    def add_opening(
165        self, opening_boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], name: str | None = None
166    ) -> "Opening":
167        """
168        Add an opening (skylight, hatch, etc.) in the roof.
169
170        Args:
171            opening_boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates for opening
172            name: Optional name for the opening
173
174        Returns:
175            The created Opening object
176        """
177        from bimascode.architecture.opening import Opening
178
179        opening = Opening(
180            host_element=self,
181            boundary=opening_boundary,
182            depth=self.thickness + 2,  # +2mm for clean cut
183            name=name or f"Opening_{len(self._openings) + 1}",
184        )
185        self._openings.append(opening)
186        self.invalidate_geometry()
187        return opening
188
189    def remove_opening(self, opening: "Opening") -> None:
190        """
191        Remove an opening from this roof.
192
193        Args:
194            opening: Opening to remove
195        """
196        if opening in self._openings:
197            self._openings.remove(opening)
198            self.invalidate_geometry()
199
200    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey):
201        """
202        Export roof to IFC.
203
204        Args:
205            ifc_file: IFC file object
206            ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place roof in
207
208        Returns:
209            IfcRoof entity
210        """
211        # Create roof
212        ifc_roof = ifc_file.create_entity(
213            "IfcRoof",
214            GlobalId=self.guid,
215            Name=self.name,
216            Description=f"{self.type.name} roof",
217            PredefinedType="FLAT_ROOF",
218        )
219
220        # Set placement (at level elevation)
221        centroid = self.get_centroid()
222        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((float(centroid[0]), float(centroid[1]), 0.0))
223
224        axis_placement = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(
225            location,
226            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0)),
227            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((1.0, 0.0, 0.0)),
228        )
229
230        local_placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
231            ifc_building_storey.ObjectPlacement, axis_placement
232        )
233
234        ifc_roof.ObjectPlacement = local_placement
235
236        # Create geometry representation using BREP
237        geom = self.get_geometry()
238
239        if geom:
240            from bimascode.export.ifc_geometry import build123d_to_ifc_brep
241
242            # Convert build123d geometry to IFC BREP
243            ifc_brep = build123d_to_ifc_brep(geom, ifc_file)
244
245            if ifc_brep:
246                # Create shape representation with BREP
247                shape_representation = ifc_file.createIfcShapeRepresentation(
248                    ifc_file.by_type("IfcGeometricRepresentationContext")[0],
249                    "Body",
250                    "Brep",
251                    [ifc_brep],
252                )
253
254                # Create product definition shape
255                product_shape = ifc_file.createIfcProductDefinitionShape(
256                    None, None, [shape_representation]
257                )
258
259                ifc_roof.Representation = product_shape
260
261        # Associate with building storey
262        ifc_file.createIfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure(
263            self._generate_guid(),
264            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
265            f"Roof{self.name}Container",
266            None,
267            [ifc_roof],
268            ifc_building_storey,
269        )
270
271        # Associate with material layer set
272        material_layer_set = self.type.to_ifc(ifc_file)
273
274        ifc_file.createIfcRelAssociatesMaterial(
275            self._generate_guid(),
276            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
277            None,
278            None,
279            [ifc_roof],
280            material_layer_set,
281        )
282
283        return ifc_roof
284
285    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox:
286        """Get axis-aligned bounding box for this roof.
287
288        Returns:
289            BoundingBox encompassing the roof geometry
290        """
291        return BoundingBox.from_polygon_2d(
292            self.boundary, self.level.elevation_mm, self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness
293        )
294
295    def __repr__(self) -> str:
296        return (
297            f"Roof(name='{self.name}', type='{self.type.name}', "
298            f"area={self.area_m2:.2f}m², slope={self.slope:.2f}°)"
299        )

A flat roof element.

Roofs are similar to floors but located at the top of a building. They support slope for drainage (typically 1-2%).

Note: Sprint 2 supports flat roofs only. Pitched roofs are P1.

Roof( roof_type: FloorType, boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], level: Level, slope: float = 0.0, name: str | None = None) 
32    def __init__(
33        self,
34        roof_type: FloorType,  # Reuse FloorType for roof assemblies
35        boundary: list[tuple[float, float]],
36        level: Level,
37        slope: float = 0.0,
38        name: str | None = None,
39    ):
40        """
41        Create a flat roof.
42
43        Args:
44            roof_type: FloorType defining the roof assembly
45            boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates defining roof boundary
46            level: Level this roof sits on
47            slope: Roof slope in degrees (for drainage, typically 1-2%)
48            name: Optional name for this roof
49        """
50        super().__init__(roof_type, name)
51
52        self.level = level
53
54        # Openings in this roof
55        self._openings: list[Opening] = []
56
57        # Store geometric parameters
58        self.set_parameter("boundary", boundary, override=False)
59        self.set_parameter("slope", slope, override=False)
60
61        # Register with level
62        if hasattr(level, "add_element"):
63            level.add_element(self)

 85    @property
 86    def area(self) -> float:
 87        """
 88        Calculate roof area in square millimeters.
 89
 90        Uses the Shoelace formula for polygon area.
 91        """
 92        boundary = self.boundary
 93        if len(boundary) < 3:
 94            return 0.0
 95
 96        # Shoelace formula
 97        area = 0.0
 98        n = len(boundary)
 99        for i in range(n):
100            j = (i + 1) % n
101            area += boundary[i][0] * boundary[j][1]
102            area -= boundary[j][0] * boundary[i][1]
103
104        return abs(area) / 2.0

def get_centroid(self) -> tuple[float, float]: 
111    def get_centroid(self) -> tuple[float, float]:
112        """
113        Calculate the centroid of the roof boundary.
114
115        Returns:
116            (x, y) coordinates of centroid
117        """
118        boundary = self.boundary
119        if len(boundary) == 0:
120            return (0.0, 0.0)
121
122        x_sum = sum(p[0] for p in boundary)
123        y_sum = sum(p[1] for p in boundary)
124        n = len(boundary)
125
126        return (x_sum / n, y_sum / n)

def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]: 
128    def get_center_3d(self) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
129        """
130        Get the 3D center point of the roof.
131

133            (x, y, z) coordinates of roof center
134        """
135        cx, cy = self.get_centroid()
136        z = self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness / 2
137        return (cx, cy, z)

151        Set the roof slope.

def add_opening( self, opening_boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], name: str | None = None) -> bimascode.architecture.opening.Opening: 
164    def add_opening(
165        self, opening_boundary: list[tuple[float, float]], name: str | None = None
166    ) -> "Opening":
167        """
168        Add an opening (skylight, hatch, etc.) in the roof.
169
170        Args:
171            opening_boundary: List of (x, y) coordinates for opening
172            name: Optional name for the opening
173
174        Returns:
175            The created Opening object
176        """
177        from bimascode.architecture.opening import Opening
178
179        opening = Opening(
180            host_element=self,
181            boundary=opening_boundary,
182            depth=self.thickness + 2,  # +2mm for clean cut

184        )

def remove_opening(self, opening: bimascode.architecture.opening.Opening) -> None:

190        """

def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey): 
200    def to_ifc(self, ifc_file, ifc_building_storey):
201        """
202        Export roof to IFC.
203
204        Args:
205            ifc_file: IFC file object
206            ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place roof in
207
208        Returns:
209            IfcRoof entity
210        """
211        # Create roof
212        ifc_roof = ifc_file.create_entity(
213            "IfcRoof",
214            GlobalId=self.guid,
215            Name=self.name,
216            Description=f"{self.type.name} roof",
217            PredefinedType="FLAT_ROOF",
218        )
219
220        # Set placement (at level elevation)
221        centroid = self.get_centroid()
222        location = ifc_file.createIfcCartesianPoint((float(centroid[0]), float(centroid[1]), 0.0))
223
224        axis_placement = ifc_file.createIfcAxis2Placement3D(
225            location,
226            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((0.0, 0.0, 1.0)),
227            ifc_file.createIfcDirection((1.0, 0.0, 0.0)),
228        )
229
230        local_placement = ifc_file.createIfcLocalPlacement(
231            ifc_building_storey.ObjectPlacement, axis_placement
232        )
233
234        ifc_roof.ObjectPlacement = local_placement
235
236        # Create geometry representation using BREP
237        geom = self.get_geometry()
238
239        if geom:
240            from bimascode.export.ifc_geometry import build123d_to_ifc_brep
241
242            # Convert build123d geometry to IFC BREP
243            ifc_brep = build123d_to_ifc_brep(geom, ifc_file)
244
245            if ifc_brep:
246                # Create shape representation with BREP
247                shape_representation = ifc_file.createIfcShapeRepresentation(
248                    ifc_file.by_type("IfcGeometricRepresentationContext")[0],
249                    "Body",
250                    "Brep",
251                    [ifc_brep],
252                )
253
254                # Create product definition shape
255                product_shape = ifc_file.createIfcProductDefinitionShape(
256                    None, None, [shape_representation]
257                )
258
259                ifc_roof.Representation = product_shape
260
261        # Associate with building storey
262        ifc_file.createIfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure(
263            self._generate_guid(),
264            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
265            f"Roof{self.name}Container",
266            None,
267            [ifc_roof],
268            ifc_building_storey,
269        )
270
271        # Associate with material layer set
272        material_layer_set = self.type.to_ifc(ifc_file)
273
274        ifc_file.createIfcRelAssociatesMaterial(
275            self._generate_guid(),
276            self.level.building._ifc_owner_history,
277            None,
278            None,
279            [ifc_roof],
280            material_layer_set,
281        )
282
283        return ifc_roof

Export roof to IFC.

Args: ifc_file: IFC file object ifc_building_storey: IFC building storey to place roof in

Returns: IfcRoof entity

def get_bounding_box(self) -> bimascode.performance.bounding_box.BoundingBox: 
285    def get_bounding_box(self) -> BoundingBox:
286        """Get axis-aligned bounding box for this roof.
287
288        Returns:
289            BoundingBox encompassing the roof geometry
290        """
291        return BoundingBox.from_polygon_2d(
292            self.boundary, self.level.elevation_mm, self.level.elevation_mm + self.thickness
293        )

Get axis-aligned bounding box for this roof.

Returns: BoundingBox encompassing the roof geometry

class Layer: 
58class Layer:
59    """
60    A single material layer in a wall assembly.
61
62    Each layer has a material, thickness, and functional role.
63    Layers are stacked from exterior to interior.
64    """
65
66    def __init__(
67        self,
68        material: Material,
69        thickness: Length | float,
70        function: LayerFunction = LayerFunction.OTHER,
71        structural: bool = False,
72        description: str | None = None,
73    ):
74        """
75        Create a material layer.
76
77        Args:
78            material: Material for this layer
79            thickness: Layer thickness (in mm or Length)
80            function: Functional role of this layer
81            structural: Whether this layer is load-bearing
82            description: Optional description
83        """
84        self.material = material
85        self.thickness = normalize_length(thickness)
86        self.function = function
87        self.structural = structural
88        self.description = description or f"{material.name} - {function.value}"
89
90    @property
91    def thickness_mm(self) -> float:
92        """Get thickness in millimeters."""
93        return self.thickness.mm
94
95    def __repr__(self) -> str:
96        return f"Layer({self.material.name}, {self.thickness_mm}mm, {self.function.value})"

A single material layer in a wall assembly.

Each layer has a material, thickness, and functional role. Layers are stacked from exterior to interior.

Layer( material: bimascode.utils.materials.Material, thickness: Length | float, function: LayerFunction = <LayerFunction.OTHER: 'Other'>, structural: bool = False, description: str | None = None) 
66    def __init__(
67        self,
68        material: Material,
69        thickness: Length | float,
70        function: LayerFunction = LayerFunction.OTHER,
71        structural: bool = False,
72        description: str | None = None,
73    ):
74        """
75        Create a material layer.
76
77        Args:
78            material: Material for this layer
79            thickness: Layer thickness (in mm or Length)
80            function: Functional role of this layer
81            structural: Whether this layer is load-bearing
82            description: Optional description
83        """
84        self.material = material
85        self.thickness = normalize_length(thickness)
86        self.function = function
87        self.structural = structural
88        self.description = description or f"{material.name} - {function.value}"

Create a material layer.

Args: material: Material for this layer thickness: Layer thickness (in mm or Length) function: Functional role of this layer structural: Whether this layer is load-bearing description: Optional description

material
thickness
function
structural
description
thickness_mm: float 
90    @property
91    def thickness_mm(self) -> float:
92        """Get thickness in millimeters."""
93        return self.thickness.mm

Get thickness in millimeters.

class LayerFunction(enum.Enum): 
42class LayerFunction(Enum):
43    """
44    Functional role of a material layer in a wall assembly.
45    """
46
47    STRUCTURE = "Structure"  # Load-bearing layer
48    SUBSTRATE = "Substrate"  # Backing/sheathing
49    THERMAL_INSULATION = "Thermal Insulation"
50    AIR_BARRIER = "Air Barrier"
51    VAPOR_BARRIER = "Vapor Barrier"
52    FINISH_EXTERIOR = "Finish - Exterior"
53    FINISH_INTERIOR = "Finish - Interior"
54    MEMBRANE = "Membrane"  # Waterproofing, etc.
55    OTHER = "Other"

Functional role of a material layer in a wall assembly.

STRUCTURE = <LayerFunction.STRUCTURE: 'Structure'>
SUBSTRATE = <LayerFunction.SUBSTRATE: 'Substrate'>
THERMAL_INSULATION = <LayerFunction.THERMAL_INSULATION: 'Thermal Insulation'>
AIR_BARRIER = <LayerFunction.AIR_BARRIER: 'Air Barrier'>
VAPOR_BARRIER = <LayerFunction.VAPOR_BARRIER: 'Vapor Barrier'>
FINISH_EXTERIOR = <LayerFunction.FINISH_EXTERIOR: 'Finish - Exterior'>
FINISH_INTERIOR = <LayerFunction.FINISH_INTERIOR: 'Finish - Interior'>
MEMBRANE = <LayerFunction.MEMBRANE: 'Membrane'>
OTHER = <LayerFunction.OTHER: 'Other'>
class UnitSystem(enum.Enum): 
12class UnitSystem(Enum):
13    """Supported unit systems."""
14
15    METRIC = "metric"
16    IMPERIAL = "imperial"

Supported unit systems.

METRIC = <UnitSystem.METRIC: 'metric'>
IMPERIAL = <UnitSystem.IMPERIAL: 'imperial'>
class LengthUnit(enum.Enum): 
19class LengthUnit(Enum):
20    """Supported length units."""
21
22    MILLIMETER = "mm"
23    CENTIMETER = "cm"
24    METER = "m"
25    INCH = "in"
26    FOOT = "ft"

Supported length units.

MILLIMETER = <LengthUnit.MILLIMETER: 'mm'>
CENTIMETER = <LengthUnit.CENTIMETER: 'cm'>
METER = <LengthUnit.METER: 'm'>
INCH = <LengthUnit.INCH: 'in'>
FOOT = <LengthUnit.FOOT: 'ft'>
class Length: 
 86class Length:
 87    """
 88    Unit-aware length measurement.
 89
 90    Stores internally in millimeters for consistency.
 91    """
 92
 93    def __init__(self, value: float, unit: str | LengthUnit = LengthUnit.MILLIMETER):
 94        """
 95        Create a length measurement.
 96
 97        Args:
 98            value: Numeric value
 99            unit: Unit of measurement (default: millimeters)
100        """
101        if isinstance(unit, str):
102            unit = LengthUnit(unit)
103
104        self._mm = value * LENGTH_TO_MM[unit]
105
106    @property
107    def mm(self) -> float:
108        """Get value in millimeters."""
109        return self._mm
110
111    @property
112    def cm(self) -> float:
113        """Get value in centimeters."""
114        return self._mm / LENGTH_TO_MM[LengthUnit.CENTIMETER]
115
116    @property
117    def m(self) -> float:
118        """Get value in meters."""
119        return self._mm / LENGTH_TO_MM[LengthUnit.METER]
120
121    @property
122    def inches(self) -> float:
123        """Get value in inches."""
124        return self._mm / LENGTH_TO_MM[LengthUnit.INCH]
125
126    @property
127    def feet(self) -> float:
128        """Get value in feet."""
129        return self._mm / LENGTH_TO_MM[LengthUnit.FOOT]
130
131    def to(self, unit: str | LengthUnit) -> float:
132        """
133        Convert to specified unit.
134
135        Args:
136            unit: Target unit
137
138        Returns:
139            Value in target unit
140        """
141        if isinstance(unit, str):
142            unit = LengthUnit(unit)
143
144        return self._mm / LENGTH_TO_MM[unit]
145
146    def __repr__(self) -> str:
147        return f"Length({self._mm} mm)"
148
149    def __str__(self) -> str:
150        return f"{self._mm} mm"
151
152    def __float__(self) -> float:
153        """Return value in millimeters when cast to float."""
154        return self._mm
155
156    def __add__(self, other):
157        if isinstance(other, Length):
158            return Length(self._mm + other._mm, LengthUnit.MILLIMETER)
159        elif isinstance(other, (int, float)):
160            return Length(self._mm + other, LengthUnit.MILLIMETER)
161        return NotImplemented
162
163    def __sub__(self, other):
164        if isinstance(other, Length):
165            return Length(self._mm - other._mm, LengthUnit.MILLIMETER)
166        elif isinstance(other, (int, float)):
167            return Length(self._mm - other, LengthUnit.MILLIMETER)
168        return NotImplemented
169
170    def __mul__(self, other):
171        if isinstance(other, (int, float)):
172            return Length(self._mm * other, LengthUnit.MILLIMETER)
173        return NotImplemented
174
175    def __truediv__(self, other):
176        if isinstance(other, (int, float)):
177            return Length(self._mm / other, LengthUnit.MILLIMETER)
178        elif isinstance(other, Length):
179            return self._mm / other._mm
180        return NotImplemented
181
182    def __eq__(self, other):
183        if isinstance(other, Length):
184            return abs(self._mm - other._mm) < 1e-6
185        return False
186
187    def __lt__(self, other):
188        if isinstance(other, Length):
189            return self._mm < other._mm
190        return NotImplemented
191
192    def __le__(self, other):
193        if isinstance(other, Length):
194            return self._mm <= other._mm
195        return NotImplemented
196
197    def __gt__(self, other):
198        if isinstance(other, Length):
199            return self._mm > other._mm
200        return NotImplemented
201
202    def __ge__(self, other):
203        if isinstance(other, Length):
204            return self._mm >= other._mm
205        return NotImplemented

Unit-aware length measurement.

Stores internally in millimeters for consistency.

Length( value: float, unit: str | LengthUnit = <LengthUnit.MILLIMETER: 'mm'>)

 94        """
 95        Create a length measurement.
 96

 99            unit: Unit of measurement (default: millimeters)
100        """
101        if isinstance(unit, str):
102            unit = LengthUnit(unit)
103
104        self._mm = value * LENGTH_TO_MM[unit]

class Area: 
208class Area:
209    """
210    Unit-aware area measurement.
211
212    Stores internally in square millimeters for consistency.
213    """
214
215    def __init__(self, value: float, unit: str | AreaUnit = AreaUnit.SQUARE_MILLIMETER):
216        """
217        Create an area measurement.
218
219        Args:
220            value: Numeric value
221            unit: Unit of measurement (default: square millimeters)
222        """
223        if isinstance(unit, str):
224            unit = AreaUnit(unit)
225
226        self._mm2 = value * AREA_TO_MM2[unit]
227
228    @property
229    def mm2(self) -> float:
230        """Get value in square millimeters."""
231        return self._mm2
232
233    @property
234    def m2(self) -> float:
235        """Get value in square meters."""
236        return self._mm2 / AREA_TO_MM2[AreaUnit.SQUARE_METER]
237
238    @property
239    def square_inches(self) -> float:
240        """Get value in square inches."""
241        return self._mm2 / AREA_TO_MM2[AreaUnit.SQUARE_INCH]
242
243    @property
244    def square_feet(self) -> float:
245        """Get value in square feet."""
246        return self._mm2 / AREA_TO_MM2[AreaUnit.SQUARE_FOOT]
247
248    def to(self, unit: str | AreaUnit) -> float:
249        """
250        Convert to specified unit.
251
252        Args:
253            unit: Target unit
254
255        Returns:
256            Value in target unit
257        """
258        if isinstance(unit, str):
259            unit = AreaUnit(unit)
260
261        return self._mm2 / AREA_TO_MM2[unit]
262
263    def __repr__(self) -> str:
264        return f"Area({self._mm2} mm²)"
265
266    def __str__(self) -> str:
267        return f"{self._mm2} mm²"

class Volume: 
270class Volume:
271    """
272    Unit-aware volume measurement.
273
274    Stores internally in cubic millimeters for consistency.
275    """
276
277    def __init__(self, value: float, unit: str | VolumeUnit = VolumeUnit.CUBIC_MILLIMETER):
278        """
279        Create a volume measurement.
280
281        Args:
282            value: Numeric value
283            unit: Unit of measurement (default: cubic millimeters)
284        """
285        if isinstance(unit, str):
286            unit = VolumeUnit(unit)
287
288        self._mm3 = value * VOLUME_TO_MM3[unit]
289
290    @property
291    def mm3(self) -> float:
292        """Get value in cubic millimeters."""
293        return self._mm3
294
295    @property
296    def m3(self) -> float:
297        """Get value in cubic meters."""
298        return self._mm3 / VOLUME_TO_MM3[VolumeUnit.CUBIC_METER]
299
300    @property
301    def cubic_inches(self) -> float:
302        """Get value in cubic inches."""
303        return self._mm3 / VOLUME_TO_MM3[VolumeUnit.CUBIC_INCH]
304
305    @property
306    def cubic_feet(self) -> float:
307        """Get value in cubic feet."""
308        return self._mm3 / VOLUME_TO_MM3[VolumeUnit.CUBIC_FOOT]
309
310    def to(self, unit: str | VolumeUnit) -> float:
311        """
312        Convert to specified unit.
313
314        Args:
315            unit: Target unit
316
317        Returns:
318            Value in target unit
319        """
320        if isinstance(unit, str):
321            unit = VolumeUnit(unit)
322
323        return self._mm3 / VOLUME_TO_MM3[unit]
324
325    def __repr__(self) -> str:
326        return f"Volume({self._mm3} mm³)"
327
328    def __str__(self) -> str:
329        return f"{self._mm3} mm³"

Stores internally in cubic millimeters for consistency.

class Angle: 
332class Angle:
333    """
334    Unit-aware angle measurement.
335
336    Stores internally in radians for consistency.
337    """
338
339    def __init__(self, value: float, unit: str | AngleUnit = AngleUnit.RADIAN):
340        """
341        Create an angle measurement.
342
343        Args:
344            value: Numeric value
345            unit: Unit of measurement (default: radians)
346        """
347        if isinstance(unit, str):
348            unit = AngleUnit(unit)
349
350        self._rad = value * ANGLE_TO_RAD[unit]
351
352    @property
353    def radians(self) -> float:
354        """Get value in radians."""
355        return self._rad
356
357    @property
358    def degrees(self) -> float:
359        """Get value in degrees."""
360        return self._rad / ANGLE_TO_RAD[AngleUnit.DEGREE]
361
362    def to(self, unit: str | AngleUnit) -> float:
363        """
364        Convert to specified unit.
365
366        Args:
367            unit: Target unit
368
369        Returns:
370            Value in target unit
371        """
372        if isinstance(unit, str):
373            unit = AngleUnit(unit)
374
375        return self._rad / ANGLE_TO_RAD[unit]
376
377    def __repr__(self) -> str:
378        return f"Angle({self._rad} rad)"
379
380    def __str__(self) -> str:
381        return f"{self._rad} rad"

Unit-aware angle measurement.

Stores internally in radians for consistency.

341        Create an angle measurement.
342

346        """
347        if isinstance(unit, str):
348            unit = AngleUnit(unit)
349
350        self._rad = value * ANGLE_TO_RAD[unit]
